diff --git a/tools/bake_vat.py b/tools/bake_vat.py
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e7af295
--- /dev/null
+++ b/tools/bake_vat.py
@@ -0,0 +1,265 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env python
+"""Offline VAT (vertex-animation-texture) baker — Blender headless.
+
+ADR-029 P2a proof spike (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#588). Bakes one or more skeletal
+clips of a skinned glb into a float-precision position texture + a UV2-vertex-
+indexed bind-pose mesh + a JSON metadata sidecar, for a MultiMeshInstance3D VAT
+horde. DISPOSABLE proof data — the permanent baker-home (Blender vs Godot-native)
+is a P2b decision (see .totem/specs/p2a-vat-spike.md).
+
+Run via the pnpm wrapper:  pnpm bake:vat
+or directly:
+  "<blender>" --background --factory-startup --python tools/bake_vat.py -- \
+      --glb apps/game-godot/assets/mini-characters/character-female-a.glb \
+      --out apps/game-godot/assets/vat-proof \
+      --clips walk,die
+
+Output (under --out):
+  <stem>.vat.exr   RGBA float position texture (width=vertex_count, height=Σframes)
+  <stem>.vat.glb   bind-pose mesh; UV2 (TEXCOORD_1) carries (vertex_index+0.5)/N
+  <stem>.vat.json  metadata (vertex/frame counts, fps, clip rows, bounds, hash, axes)
+
+Contracts (cohort folds, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#588):
+  - FLOAT texture (EXR) — no 8-bit quantization → no vertex-stepping.
+  - Root motion: clips are in-place; we bake in object-LOCAL space (excludes the
+    object transform). We DETECT root-bone horizontal travel and FAIL LOUD if a
+    clip exceeds --root-motion-eps (the sim owns translation; a moving root would
+    double-apply).
+  - Axis convert Blender Z-up -> Godot Y-up: (x, y, z) -> (x, z, -y). The texture
+    stores positions already in Godot space (the shader overwrites VERTEX with the
+    sample, so the exported mesh geometry is irrelevant; only UV2 + topology matter).
+  - Vertex addressing via UV2 (survives Godot vertex-splitting — the index travels
+    with the vertex as an attribute, unlike VERTEX_ID which indexes the post-split
+    array).
+"""
+
+import bpy
+import sys
+import os
+import json
+import math
+import hashlib
+
+
+def _argv():
+    return sys.argv[sys.argv.index("--") + 1:] if "--" in sys.argv else []
+
+
+def _parse(argv):
+    opts = {
+        "glb": "apps/game-godot/assets/mini-characters/character-female-a.glb",
+        "out": "apps/game-godot/assets/vat-proof",
+        "clips": "walk,die",
+        "root_motion_eps": 0.5,  # WARN threshold for stripped root travel (not fatal)
+    }
+    i = 0
+    while i < len(argv):
+        a = argv[i]
+        if a == "--glb":
+            opts["glb"] = argv[i + 1]; i += 2
+        elif a == "--out":
+            opts["out"] = argv[i + 1]; i += 2
+        elif a == "--clips":
+            opts["clips"] = argv[i + 1]; i += 2
+        elif a == "--root-motion-eps":
+            opts["root_motion_eps"] = float(argv[i + 1]); i += 2
+        else:
+            i += 1
+    return opts
+
+
+def _die(msg):
+    print(f"[bake_vat] ERROR: {msg}")
+    sys.exit(1)
+
+
+def main():
+    opts = _parse(_argv())
+    glb = os.path.abspath(opts["glb"])
+    out_dir = os.path.abspath(opts["out"])
+    clip_names = [c.strip() for c in opts["clips"].split(",") if c.strip()]
+    if not os.path.isfile(glb):
+        _die(f"glb not found: {glb}")
+    os.makedirs(out_dir, exist_ok=True)
+    stem = os.path.splitext(os.path.basename(glb))[0]
+
+    print(f"[bake_vat] importing {glb}")
+    bpy.ops.wm.read_factory_settings(use_empty=True)
+    bpy.ops.import_scene.gltf(filepath=glb)
+    scene = bpy.context.scene
+    fps = scene.render.fps
+
+    # --- collect the skinned meshes (have an ARMATURE modifier); drop junk ---
+    arm = next((o for o in bpy.data.objects if o.type == "ARMATURE"), None)
+    if arm is None:
+        _die("no armature in glb")
+    skinned = [o for o in bpy.data.objects
+               if o.type == "MESH"
+               and any(m.type == "ARMATURE" for m in o.modifiers)]
+    if not skinned:
+        _die("no skinned meshes (armature-modified) found")
+    # deterministic order so the bake is reproducible
+    skinned.sort(key=lambda o: o.name)
+    print(f"[bake_vat] skinned meshes: {[o.name for o in skinned]}")
+
+    # --- join into one mesh (a MultiMesh renders ONE mesh N times) ---
+    bpy.ops.object.select_all(action="DESELECT")
+    for o in skinned:
+        o.select_set(True)
+    joined = skinned[0]
+    bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = joined
+    if len(skinned) > 1:
+        bpy.ops.object.join()
+    me = joined.data
+    vcount = len(me.vertices)
+    print(f"[bake_vat] joined vertex_count={vcount}")
+
+    # --- UV2 (second layer / TEXCOORD_1) carries the vertex index ---
+    while len(me.uv_layers) < 2:
+        me.uv_layers.new(name="vat_index")
+    uv2 = me.uv_layers[1]
+    for loop in me.loops:
+        u = (loop.vertex_index + 0.5) / vcount
+        uv2.data[loop.index].uv = (u, 0.5)
+    # keep only 2 UV layers (Godot caps at UV/UV2)
+    while len(me.uv_layers) > 2:
+        me.uv_layers.remove(me.uv_layers[2])
+
+    # --- resolve actions + count rows ---
+    clips = []
+    row = 0
+    for name in clip_names:
+        act = bpy.data.actions.get(name)
+        if act is None:
+            _die(f"action {name!r} not found; available: {[a.name for a in bpy.data.actions]}")
+        f0, f1 = int(act.frame_range[0]), int(act.frame_range[1])
+        nframes = f1 - f0 + 1
+        clips.append({"name": name, "f0": f0, "f1": f1, "row_start": row,
+                      "frame_count": nframes, "action": act})
+        row += nframes
+    total_rows = row
+    print(f"[bake_vat] clips={[(c['name'], c['frame_count']) for c in clips]} total_rows={total_rows}")
+
+    arm.animation_data_create()
+    depsgraph = bpy.context.evaluated_depsgraph_get()
+
+    # --- bake: pixels are RGBA, row-major from image BOTTOM (Blender native).
+    # We write frame-row r at image-y r; the shader samples v=(r+0.5)/total_rows.
+    px = [0.0] * (vcount * total_rows * 4)
+    bmin = [1e9, 1e9, 1e9]
+    bmax = [-1e9, -1e9, -1e9]
+    # root = the parentless bone (pose.bones order isn't guaranteed root-first)
+    if not arm.pose.bones:
+        _die("armature has no bones")
+    root_bone = next((b for b in arm.pose.bones if b.parent is None), arm.pose.bones[0])
+
+    # Root motion is STRIPPED: each frame we anchor the root bone's HORIZONTAL
+    # world position (Blender X/Y) at origin, so the clip plays in-place at the
+    # agent's sim position. Vertical (Z, up) is kept (bob, fall, collapse). This
+    # honors "the sim owns translation"; locomotion drift would double-apply.
+    for c in clips:
+        arm.animation_data.action = c["action"]
+        stripped = 0.0  # pre-strip horizontal travel, reported for info
+        rest = None
+        for fi in range(c["frame_count"]):
+            f = c["f0"] + fi
+            scene.frame_set(f)
+            depsgraph = bpy.context.evaluated_depsgraph_get()
+            rb = arm.matrix_world @ root_bone.head  # world-space root head
+            if rest is None:
+                rest = rb.copy()
+            else:
+                stripped = max(stripped, math.hypot(rb.x - rest.x, rb.y - rest.y))
+            obj_eval = joined.evaluated_get(depsgraph)
+            mw = obj_eval.matrix_world
+            mesh_eval = obj_eval.to_mesh()
+            r = c["row_start"] + fi
+            base = r * vcount * 4
+            for i, v in enumerate(mesh_eval.vertices):
+                w = mw @ v.co  # world-space deformed vertex (Blender Z-up)
+                # strip root: re-express horizontal relative to the root each frame
+                bx = w.x - rb.x
+                by = w.y - rb.y
+                bz = w.z
+                # Blender Z-up -> Godot Y-up
+                gx, gy, gz = bx, bz, -by
+                o = base + i * 4
+                px[o + 0] = gx
+                px[o + 1] = gy
+                px[o + 2] = gz
+                px[o + 3] = 1.0
+                if gx < bmin[0]: bmin[0] = gx
+                if gy < bmin[1]: bmin[1] = gy
+                if gz < bmin[2]: bmin[2] = gz
+                if gx > bmax[0]: bmax[0] = gx
+                if gy > bmax[1]: bmax[1] = gy
+                if gz > bmax[2]: bmax[2] = gz
+            obj_eval.to_mesh_clear()
+        print(f"[bake_vat]   {c['name']}: rows [{c['row_start']},{c['row_start']+c['frame_count']}) "
+              f"stripped_root_travel={stripped:.4f}")
+        if stripped > opts["root_motion_eps"]:
+            print(f"[bake_vat]   WARNING: {c['name']} stripped {stripped:.3f} of root travel "
+                  f"(> --root-motion-eps {opts['root_motion_eps']}) — confirm the clip is in-place")
+
+    # --- write the EXR (float) ---
+    exr_path = os.path.join(out_dir, f"{stem}.vat.exr")
+    img = bpy.data.images.new(f"{stem}_vat", width=vcount, height=total_rows,
+                              float_buffer=True, alpha=True)
+    img.colorspace_settings.name = "Non-Color"
+    img.pixels = px
+    img.file_format = "OPEN_EXR"
+    img.filepath_raw = exr_path
+    scene.render.image_settings.file_format = "OPEN_EXR"
+    scene.render.image_settings.color_depth = "32"
+    scene.render.image_settings.exr_codec = "ZIP"
+    img.save()
+    print(f"[bake_vat] wrote {exr_path} ({vcount}x{total_rows} float)")
+
+    # --- export the bind-pose mesh (UV2 = index) ---
+    bpy.ops.object.select_all(action="DESELECT")
+    joined.select_set(True)
+    bpy.context.view_layer.objects.active = joined
+    # neutralize deformation for the exported geometry (positions are overwritten
+    # by the shader anyway; this keeps the bind-pose tidy)
+    scene.frame_set(0)
+    glb_path = os.path.join(out_dir, f"{stem}.vat.glb")
+    bpy.ops.export_scene.gltf(
+        filepath=glb_path,
+        use_selection=True,
+        export_format="GLB",
+        export_yup=True,
+        export_animations=False,
+        export_skins=False,
+        export_apply=False,
+    )
+    print(f"[bake_vat] wrote {glb_path}")
+
+    # --- metadata sidecar ---
+    with open(glb, "rb") as fh:
+        src_hash = hashlib.md5(fh.read()).hexdigest()
+    meta = {
+        "schema": "vat-proof-v0",
+        "source_glb": os.path.relpath(glb, os.getcwd()).replace("\\", "/"),
+        "source_md5": src_hash,
+        "vertex_count": vcount,
+        "total_rows": total_rows,
+        "fps": fps,
+        "clips": [{"name": c["name"], "row_start": c["row_start"],
+                   "frame_count": c["frame_count"]} for c in clips],
+        "bounds": {"min": bmin, "max": bmax},
+        "coord_basis": "godot-y-up",
+        "axis_convert": "blender(x,y,z)->godot(x,z,-y)",
+        "root_motion": "stripped (horizontal root anchored per-frame; vertical kept)",
+        "uv2": "(vertex_index+0.5)/vertex_count in TEXCOORD_1",
+        "texture_format": "OPEN_EXR RGBA 32f, Non-Color",
+        "row_origin": "row r at image-y r (bottom-up); shader v=(r+0.5)/total_rows",
+    }
+    json_path = os.path.join(out_dir, f"{stem}.vat.json")
+    with open(json_path, "w") as fh:
+        json.dump(meta, fh, indent=2)
+    print(f"[bake_vat] wrote {json_path}")
+    print("[bake_vat] DONE")
+
+
+if __name__ == "__main__":
+    main()

diff --git a/adr/adr-029-3d-render-migration.md b/adr/adr-029-3d-render-migration.md
index 351ed60..bb29349 100644
--- a/adr/adr-029-3d-render-migration.md
+++ b/adr/adr-029-3d-render-migration.md
@@ -56,6 +56,10 @@ The migration is **broad-surface but low-core-risk**: rendering is downstream of
 - **Migration-window length — DECIDED (satur8d 2026-06-19): no 2D fallback tail.** 2D is retained ONLY as a Tenet-7 build scaffold to keep `main` green across the migration PRs (P1–P4), and is **deleted at the flip (P5)** — no post-flip fallback flag is retained. This overrides the cohort's "keep a 2D fallback flag ~1 minor before deletion" recommendation; the migration window collapses to "as fast as the work lands," gated only by fixture-backed visual parity (§2 contract 9).
 - **Cheap-wins re-sequencing (`mmnto-ai/liquid-city#555` / `mmnto-ai/liquid-city#556` / `mmnto-ai/liquid-city#557`) — DECIDED (satur8d 2026-06-19):** re-homed by visibility/durability, not run as a phase-0 batch. **`mmnto-ai/liquid-city#557` (speed) shipped first** (standalone sim-side, merged `7ff0d26`). **`mmnto-ai/liquid-city#555` (player-visual) folds into `mmnto-ai/liquid-city#558` / P1** — the rigged 3D player IS the legibility fix; no throwaway 2D. **`mmnto-ai/liquid-city#556` (loft) defers to phase 2** with the 3D horde (z-loft only reads once the perspective Camera3D + 3D horde land; tuning under top-down = blind calibration).
 
+## Amendments
+
+- **3D became the working default at P2b PR3 (satur8d 2026-06-22) — ahead of the planned P5 flip.** PR `mmnto-ai/liquid-city#597` set `use_3d_camera = true` by default. The §1 phased sequence flips the default only at **P5** on fixture-backed visual parity (§2 contract 9), with 2D as the working default through P1–P4; this moves the boot default forward. Rationale: by P2b PR3 the 3D path renders the player + the production VAT horde, and the operator chose 3D as the default working view ("2D only for debugging") rather than continue booting into 2D. **This does NOT collapse the P5 gate.** 2D is still retained as the Tenet-7 build scaffold (NOT deleted — the no-2D-fallback deletion still waits for P5's fixture-backed visual-parity gate, §Resolved Operator Calls), and `use_3d_camera` remains an operator toggle. Only the boot DEFAULT changed (3D-first); the parity gate and the 2D-deletion trigger are unchanged. P5 still stands: prove fixture-backed visual parity → delete 2D.
+
 ## Non-goals
 
 Touching the deterministic sim; multi-floor camera altitude tracking (Proposal 030 defers it); a big-bang rewrite.
diff --git a/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd b/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..1ca8856
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,415 @@
+class_name HordeRenderAdapter
+extends RefCounted
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR3 — PRODUCTION 3D horde render adapter (Surface A consumer +
+# CUSTOM_DATA v1). Successor to the disposable proof `VatHordeAdapter`.
+#
+# Reads TWO FFI buffers each frame and assembles one stride-16 MultiMesh3D
+# buffer behind `use_3d_camera`:
+#   - get_agent_buffer()             — stride-8 Transform2D (position + heading)
+#   - get_agent_render_state_buffer()— stride-4 render-state record per slot
+#       [0] flags (LIVE/BALLISTIC + reserved gore), [1] health, [2] hitstop,
+#       [3] AnimIntent.
+#
+# Per slot it derives CUSTOM_DATA v1: x = per-slot anim clock (FROZEN while
+# hitstop > 0 — HitStop is the adapter not advancing the clock; the shader
+# never reads global TIME), y = clip index (AnimIntent → clip name → numeric
+# index from the bake table), z = health fraction, w = the packed flags uint
+# (floatBitsToUint round-trip proven bit-exact by spike 1).
+#
+# Boundaries (spec .totem/specs/p2b-3d-horde.md):
+#   - Owns the MultiMesh + buffer assembly + per-slot clocks. NEVER queries
+#     the sim FFI — sim_driver feeds it both buffers each frame.
+#   - Headless -> no-op (DisplayServer guard); rendering needs a real GPU.
+#   - Clocks keyed by stable BufferSlot; dead slots (zeroed transform) masked.
+
+const SHADER_PATH := "res://shaders/horde_render.gdshader"
+const VAT_DIR := "res://assets/vat-proof/"
+const VAT_STEM := "character-female-a"
+const AGENT_STRIDE := 8  # sim get_agent_buffer() Transform2D stride
+const OUT_STRIDE := 16  # MultiMesh3D: 12 Transform3D + 4 CUSTOM_DATA
+
+# AnimIntent — MIRROR of `density_feedback.rs` `AnimIntent` (the wire contract).
+# Locked against the Rust discriminants by tests/anim_intent_parity.rs.
+const ANIM_IDLE := 0
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_STAGGER := 2
+const ANIM_BALLISTIC := 3
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+const ANIM_ATTACK := 5
+const ANIM_INTENT_COUNT := 6
+
+# Render-state record layout — MIRROR of `density_feedback.rs`
+# RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + RS_* offsets.
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const RS_FLAGS := 0
+const RS_HEALTH := 1
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const RS_INTENT := 3
+
+var _render_root: Node3D
+var _headless := false
+var _enabled := false
+var _ready := false
+## Production horde shows the real Kenney colormap texture by default (ADR-029
+## P2b PR3). The disposable proof defaulted this OFF for a clean perf baseline;
+## the production look wants the material. One UV texture sample — negligible.
+var _colormap_enabled := true
+## Dmg-based red health tint. OFF by default (clean Kenney textures for the
+## artist preview); enabled via `set_health_tint_enabled` (the `--health-tint`
+## launch flag wires it on for an A/B look). ADR-029 P2b PR3.
+var _health_tint_enabled := false
+
+var _mmi: MultiMeshInstance3D
+var _mm: MultiMesh
+var _mat: ShaderMaterial
+var _meta: Dictionary = {}
+
+# per-slot anim clock (indexed by stable BufferSlot)
+var _clock := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _was_live := PackedByteArray()
+var _out := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _scratch := PackedByteArray()  # 4-byte bitcast scratch for the w pack
+
+# AnimIntent -> clip row index (built from the bake clip table at setup).
+var _intent_clip := PackedInt32Array()
+
+# tunables (calibrated from the proof asset; shared with VatHordeAdapter)
+var character_scale := 1.0
+var y_offset := 0.19
+var facing_offset := 0.0
+
+# perf instrumentation
+var last_assembly_usec := 0
+var last_live := 0
+
+
+func _init(render_root: Node3D, headless: bool) -> void:
+	_render_root = render_root
+	_headless = headless
+	_scratch.resize(4)
+
+
+# Loads the VAT asset + builds the MultiMesh + the intent→clip table. Returns
+# false (no-op) when headless or on a load failure. Idempotent.
+func setup() -> bool:
+	if _ready:
+		return true
+	if _headless:
+		return false
+	# Runtime-load the source EXR at full RGBAF precision (NOT the imported
+	# .ctex, which VRAM-compresses / may half-float and step the vertices).
+	# load_from_file accepts res:// directly (FileAccess maps it to the project
+	# dir in editor/harness — probe-verified RGBAF, identical to a globalized
+	# path), so do NOT globalize_path: that yields an absolute OS path that can
+	# never resolve inside an exported .pck. NOTE: neither form is export-safe
+	# yet — the raw .exr is not packed into an export (imported assets ship as
+	# .ctex); the export-safe path is the deferred baked .res ImageTexture
+	# (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#599). This adapter is editor/harness-only today.
+	var img := Image.load_from_file(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + ".vat.exr")
+	if img == null:
+		push_error("HordeRenderAdapter: failed to load VAT EXR")
+		return false
+	if img.get_format() != Image.FORMAT_RGBAF:
+		img.convert(Image.FORMAT_RGBAF)
+	var tex := ImageTexture.create_from_image(img)
+
+	var meta_str := FileAccess.get_file_as_string(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + ".vat.json")
+	# JSON.parse_string returns null on malformed input; assigning null to the
+	# typed `_meta: Dictionary` would crash. Type-check before the assign so a
+	# corrupt sidecar fails loud via the is_empty() guard, not a Nil call.
+	var parsed: Variant = JSON.parse_string(meta_str) if meta_str != "" else {}
+	_meta = parsed if parsed is Dictionary else {}
+	if _meta.is_empty():
+		push_error("HordeRenderAdapter: failed to load VAT metadata (missing or malformed JSON)")
+		return false
+
+	var mesh := _load_mesh()
+	if mesh == null:
+		push_error("HordeRenderAdapter: failed to load VAT mesh")
+		return false
+
+	var shader := load(SHADER_PATH) as Shader
+	if shader == null:
+		push_error("HordeRenderAdapter: failed to load shader")
+		return false
+	_mat = ShaderMaterial.new()
+	_mat.shader = shader
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("vat_tex", tex)
+	var cm := load(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + "_colormap.png") as Texture2D
+	if cm != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("colormap", cm)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", _colormap_enabled)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", _health_tint_enabled)
+	# The .get default only fires when the key is ABSENT; a literal
+	# "total_rows": 0 from a corrupt bake would otherwise reach the shader and
+	# project every vertex to infinity → silently invisible horde (CR, #597).
+	# Clamp to >= 1 and warn loudly so a bad sidecar is diagnosable, not silent.
+	var raw_rows := int(_meta.get("total_rows", 1))
+	if raw_rows < 1:
+		push_warning(
+			(
+				"HordeRenderAdapter: total_rows=%d (<1) in bake meta — clamping to 1; check the sidecar for a corrupt bake."
+				% raw_rows
+			)
+		)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("total_rows", maxi(raw_rows, 1))
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("fps", float(_meta.get("fps", 24.0)))
+	# A malformed sidecar could carry a non-Array under "clips"; the typed
+	# assignment would then crash, so coerce to [] and let the guards below run.
+	var raw_clips: Variant = _meta.get("clips", [])
+	var clips: Array = raw_clips if raw_clips is Array else []
+	# clip_count guards both ends: mini(...,8) because the shader arrays are [8],
+	# and maxi(...,1) because an empty/absent clip table would otherwise be 0 →
+	# the shader's `clamp(x, 0, clip_count-1)` underflows to -1 and indexes
+	# clip_row_start[-1] (same class as the total_rows guard above, CR #597).
+	var clip_count := maxi(mini(clips.size(), 8), 1)
+	if clips.is_empty():
+		push_warning(
+			"HordeRenderAdapter: bake meta has no clips — clamping clip_count to 1; check the sidecar."
+		)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("clip_count", clip_count)
+	var starts := PackedFloat32Array()
+	var counts := PackedFloat32Array()
+	starts.resize(8)
+	counts.resize(8)
+	# Unused rows default to a safe single-frame count, not 0 — a zero frame
+	# count would make the shader's clip duration collapse (CR #597).
+	counts.fill(1.0)
+	for i in clips.size():
+		if i >= 8:
+			break
+		# Guard non-Dictionary elements (corrupt sidecar): .get() on a non-Dict
+		# crashes; default such rows to clip 0 / single-frame.
+		var clip_data: Variant = clips[i]
+		if clip_data is Dictionary:
+			starts[i] = float(clip_data.get("row_start", 0))
+			# Clamp frame_count >= 1 so an explicit "frame_count": 0 can't reach
+			# the shader as a zero-length clip (CR #597).
+			counts[i] = maxf(float(clip_data.get("frame_count", 1)), 1.0)
+		else:
+			starts[i] = 0.0
+			counts[i] = 1.0
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("clip_row_start", starts)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("clip_frame_count", counts)
+	_build_intent_clip_table(clips)
+
+	_mm = MultiMesh.new()
+	_mm.transform_format = MultiMesh.TRANSFORM_3D
+	_mm.use_custom_data = true
+	_mm.mesh = mesh
+	# the shader displaces VERTEX, so the bind-pose AABB understates extent —
+	# give a generous custom AABB so instances don't cull mid-animation.
+	mesh.custom_aabb = AABB(Vector3(-1.5, -1.5, -1.5), Vector3(3.0, 3.0, 3.0))
+
+	_mmi = MultiMeshInstance3D.new()
+	_mmi.name = "HordeRender"
+	_mmi.multimesh = _mm
+	_mmi.material_override = _mat
+	_mmi.visible = false
+	_render_root.add_child(_mmi)
+
+	_ready = true
+	return true
+
+
+# Map each AnimIntent to a clip row index by NAME preference, falling back to
+# clip 0 (the spec's "Godot maps anim_intent → clip name → numeric index from
+# the bake metadata"). Unknown names resolve to 0 so a sparse proof asset
+# (walk + die) still renders every intent without an out-of-range clip.
+func _build_intent_clip_table(clips: Array) -> void:
+	_intent_clip.resize(ANIM_INTENT_COUNT)
+	_intent_clip.fill(0)
+	var by_name := {}
+	for i in clips.size():
+		var clip_data: Variant = clips[i]
+		if not (clip_data is Dictionary):
+			continue
+		var nm := String(clip_data.get("name", ""))
+		if nm != "":
+			by_name[nm] = i
+	# preference lists per intent (first present name wins; else clip 0)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_IDLE] = _first_clip(by_name, ["idle", "walk"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION] = _first_clip(by_name, ["walk", "run", "locomotion"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_STAGGER] = _first_clip(by_name, ["hit", "stagger", "walk"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_BALLISTIC] = _first_clip(by_name, ["fall", "flail", "walk"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH] = _first_clip(by_name, ["die", "death", "walk"])
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_ATTACK] = _first_clip(by_name, ["attack", "walk"])
+
+
+func _first_clip(by_name: Dictionary, names: Array) -> int:
+	for nm in names:
+		if by_name.has(nm):
+			return int(by_name[nm])
+	return 0
+
+
+func set_active(enabled: bool) -> void:
+	_enabled = enabled
+	if _mmi != null:
+		_mmi.visible = enabled and not _headless
+
+
+func is_active() -> bool:
+	return _enabled and _ready and not _headless
+
+
+func set_colormap_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_colormap_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", on)
+
+
+func is_colormap_enabled() -> bool:
+	return _colormap_enabled
+
+
+# Toggle the dmg-based red health tint (off by default — clean textures).
+func set_health_tint_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_health_tint_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", on)
+
+
+# Per-frame: assemble the MultiMesh buffer from the stride-8 transform buffer +
+# the stride-4 render-state buffer. `render_state` may be empty/short (init
+# race / lock-poison) — missing records default to live-locomotion full-health.
+func update(
+	agent_buf: PackedFloat32Array, render_state: PackedByteArray, count: int, delta: float
+) -> void:
+	if not is_active():
+		return
+	# Defensive clamp: the loop reads agent_buf[s * AGENT_STRIDE ..]; if a
+	# desync ever hands us count > the transform buffer's capacity we'd index
+	# out of bounds and halt the script. Mirrors the short-render_state defense
+	# below. Normal path: count == agent_buf.size() / AGENT_STRIDE (no clamp).
+	var max_agents := agent_buf.size() / AGENT_STRIDE
+	if count > max_agents:
+		push_error(
+			(
+				"HordeRenderAdapter: count %d exceeds agent_buf capacity %d — clamping (FFI desync?)"
+				% [count, max_agents]
+			)
+		)
+		count = max_agents
+	var t0 := Time.get_ticks_usec()
+	_ensure_slots(count)
+	var needed := count * OUT_STRIDE
+	if _out.size() < needed:
+		_out.resize(needed)
+	var rs_len := render_state.size()
+
+	var live := 0
+	for s in count:
+		var bo := s * AGENT_STRIDE
+		var bxx := agent_buf[bo + 0]
+		var byx := agent_buf[bo + 1]
+		var ox := agent_buf[bo + 3]
+		var bxy := agent_buf[bo + 4]
+		var byy := agent_buf[bo + 5]
+		var oy := agent_buf[bo + 7]
+		# dead slots are zeroed by the snapshot writer -> all-zero basis
+		if absf(bxx) < 1e-9 and absf(bxy) < 1e-9 and absf(byx) < 1e-9 and absf(byy) < 1e-9:
+			_was_live[s] = 0
+			continue
+		if _was_live[s] == 0:
+			_clock[s] = 0.0  # (re)born / recycled slot -> fresh clock
+			_was_live[s] = 1
+
+		# render-state record (default to live-locomotion full-health if absent)
+		var ro := s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		var flags := 0
+		var health := 255
+		var hitstop := 0
+		var intent := ANIM_LOCOMOTION
+		if ro + RENDER_STATE_STRIDE <= rs_len:
+			flags = render_state[ro + RS_FLAGS]
+			health = render_state[ro + RS_HEALTH]
+			hitstop = render_state[ro + RS_HITSTOP]
+			intent = render_state[ro + RS_INTENT]
+
+		# HitStop = freeze this slot's clock; the rest keep advancing.
+		if hitstop == 0:
+			_clock[s] += delta
+
+		# sim heading (sim plane) -> Godot yaw about +Y; sim (X,Y) -> Godot (X,Z)
+		var heading := atan2(bxy, bxx)
+		var b := Basis(Vector3.UP, -heading + facing_offset)
+		if character_scale != 1.0:
+			b = b.scaled(Vector3(character_scale, character_scale, character_scale))
+		var po := live * OUT_STRIDE
+		_out[po + 0] = b.x.x
+		_out[po + 1] = b.y.x
+		_out[po + 2] = b.z.x
+		_out[po + 3] = ox
+		_out[po + 4] = b.x.y
+		_out[po + 5] = b.y.y
+		_out[po + 6] = b.z.y
+		_out[po + 7] = y_offset
+		_out[po + 8] = b.x.z
+		_out[po + 9] = b.y.z
+		_out[po + 10] = b.z.z
+		_out[po + 11] = oy
+		# CUSTOM_DATA v1: x=clock y=clip z=health_frac w=packed-flags-uint
+		_out[po + 12] = _clock[s]
+		_out[po + 13] = float(_intent_clip[clampi(intent, 0, ANIM_INTENT_COUNT - 1)])
+		_out[po + 14] = float(health) / 255.0
+		_out[po + 15] = _pack_flags(flags)
+		live += 1
+
+	_mm.instance_count = live
+	if live > 0:
+		_out.resize(live * OUT_STRIDE)
+		_mm.buffer = _out
+	last_assembly_usec = Time.get_ticks_usec() - t0
+	last_live = live
+
+
+# Bit-reinterpret the flags byte into CUSTOM_DATA.w (the shader decodes it with
+# floatBitsToUint). The value lives in the denormal float range, proven exact
+# by the floatBitsToUint round-trip spike. Higher bytes reserved (gore mask).
+func _pack_flags(flags: int) -> float:
+	_scratch.encode_u32(0, flags & 0xFFFFFFFF)
+	return _scratch.decode_float(0)
+
+
+func debug_status() -> String:
+	if not is_active():
+		return "Horde3D: off"
+	return "Horde3D: %d inst, asm %.2fms" % [last_live, last_assembly_usec / 1000.0]
+
+
+func _ensure_slots(count: int) -> void:
+	if _clock.size() >= count:
+		return
+	_clock.resize(count)
+	_was_live.resize(count)
+
+
+func _load_mesh() -> Mesh:
+	var scene := load(VAT_DIR + VAT_STEM + ".vat.glb") as PackedScene
+	if scene == null:
+		return null
+	var root := scene.instantiate()
+	if root == null:
+		return null
+	var mi := _find_mesh(root)
+	var mesh: Mesh = null
+	if mi != null:
+		mesh = mi.mesh
+	# `root` was instantiated but never add_child'd — it has no SceneTree, so
+	# queue_free() (which defers to the tree's end-of-frame sweep) would never
+	# run and the scene would leak. free() deallocates the orphan immediately.
+	# `mesh` is a Resource ref and survives the node teardown.
+	root.free()
+	return mesh
+
+
+func _find_mesh(n: Node) -> MeshInstance3D:
+	if n is MeshInstance3D:
+		return n
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		var r := _find_mesh(c)
+		if r != null:
+			return r
+	return null

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/VatAssemblyBench.gd b/apps/game-godot/VatAssemblyBench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..c99326d
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/VatAssemblyBench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,202 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR3 — SPIKE 2: production VAT assembly cost (measurement gate).
+#
+# Decides the "Rust transform graduation" fork: do we keep the Godot-side buffer
+# assembly (P2a measured 0.26ms/512 for a bare transform copy) or graduate it to a
+# Rust get_agent_buffer_3d()? That call is justified ONLY if the *production* path —
+# 400 agents, full render-state eval + clip mapping + 16-float pack — shows cost.
+# P2a only measured a bare transform copy; this measures the real per-slot work.
+#
+# PURE CPU: this times the GDScript assembly, NOT rendering or GPU upload — so it
+# runs HEADLESS (no real FFI / GPU needed). The render-state buffer is SYNTHETIC
+# (the get_agent_render_state_buffer() FFI lands in PR3 proper); the per-slot WORK
+# is faithful to what that adapter will do (decode bits -> anim_intent -> clip,
+# basis-from-heading, bitcast-pack CUSTOM_DATA.w). Honest upper bound: the number
+# here is >= production (synthetic input is dense/all-live; no early-out on culled).
+#
+# Run (headless OK):
+#   <godot> --headless --path apps/game-godot res://vat_assembly_bench.tscn
+# Prints [asm-bench] lines (min/avg/p99 usec per assembly) and quits.
+
+const AGENT_STRIDE := 8  # sim get_agent_buffer() Transform2D stride
+const OUT_STRIDE := 16  # MultiMesh3D: 12 Transform3D + 4 CUSTOM_DATA
+const ITERS := 600  # assembly iterations timed per N (drop warm-up)
+const WARMUP := 60
+const COUNTS: Array[int] = [256, 400, 512, 1000]
+
+# horde-state bit protocol (mirror of density_feedback.rs — read-only here)
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const BALLISTIC_BIT := 0x10
+const STUCK_BIT := 0x80
+const HORDE_STATE_MASK := 0x03
+
+# anim_intent enum — values mirror the canonical AnimIntent (density_feedback.rs).
+# This bench DERIVES a representative intent from synthetic flags as a
+# conservative upper bound on per-slot work; the production adapter reads the
+# precomputed RS_INTENT byte (cheaper), so the measurement over-counts if
+# anything. Values kept identical to the wire enum so a future diff against the
+# adapter stays honest (CR / Greptile P2, #597). ATTACK is reserved (unused).
+const INTENT_IDLE := 0
+const INTENT_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const INTENT_STAGGER := 2
+const INTENT_BALLISTIC := 3
+const INTENT_DEATH := 4
+const INTENT_ATTACK := 5
+
+var _agent := PackedFloat32Array()  # synthetic stride-8 transforms
+var _state := PackedByteArray()  # synthetic render-state bytes (1/slot)
+var _health := PackedByteArray()  # synthetic health bytes (0..255)
+var _hitstop := PackedByteArray()  # synthetic hitstop remaining frames
+var _out := PackedFloat32Array()  # assembly target (reused)
+var _scratch := PackedByteArray()  # 4-byte bitcast scratch for the w pack
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	_scratch.resize(4)
+	print("[asm-bench] start; headless=", DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless")
+	for n in COUNTS:
+		_run_count(n)
+	print("[asm-bench] DONE")
+	get_tree().quit(0)
+
+
+func _run_count(n: int) -> void:
+	_seed(n)
+	var samples := PackedFloat64Array()
+	samples.resize(ITERS)
+	for it in ITERS + WARMUP:
+		# vary the per-slot clock by iteration so nothing constant-folds (and the
+		# pack input changes), faithful to a live frame where clocks advance.
+		var t0 := Time.get_ticks_usec()
+		var live := _assemble(n, float(it) * 0.016)
+		var dt := Time.get_ticks_usec() - t0
+		if it >= WARMUP:
+			samples[it - WARMUP] = float(dt)
+		if it == WARMUP:
+			# sanity: report live count once (synthetic is all-live by construction)
+			_last_live = live
+	var sorted := Array(samples)
+	sorted.sort()
+	var mn: float = sorted[0]
+	var p99: float = sorted[int(floor(float(sorted.size() - 1) * 0.99))]
+	var sum := 0.0
+	for v in sorted:
+		sum += v
+	var avg := sum / float(sorted.size())
+	print(
+		(
+			"[asm-bench] N=%d live=%d min=%.1fus avg=%.1fus p99=%.1fus (%.3fms avg)"
+			% [n, _last_live, mn, avg, p99, avg / 1000.0]
+		)
+	)
+
+
+var _last_live := 0
+
+
+# One frame of production-representative assembly. Returns the live count.
+func _assemble(n: int, clock_base: float) -> int:
+	var needed := n * OUT_STRIDE
+	if _out.size() < needed:
+		_out.resize(needed)
+	var live := 0
+	for s in n:
+		var bo := s * AGENT_STRIDE
+		var bxx := _agent[bo + 0]
+		var byx := _agent[bo + 1]
+		var ox := _agent[bo + 3]
+		var bxy := _agent[bo + 4]
+		var byy := _agent[bo + 5]
+		var oy := _agent[bo + 7]
+		# dead-slot mask (zeroed basis) — same predicate the adapter uses
+		if absf(bxx) < 1e-9 and absf(bxy) < 1e-9 and absf(byx) < 1e-9 and absf(byy) < 1e-9:
+			continue
+
+		# --- render-state eval (the new per-slot work PR3 adds) ---
+		var st := _state[s]
+		var health_frac := float(_health[s]) / 255.0
+		var hitstop := _hitstop[s]
+		# Canonical priority is Death > Stagger > Ballistic > Idle
+		# (derive_anim_intent). The synthetic state byte has no Staggered bit, so
+		# hitstop > 0 proxies for stagger here — and the proxy is checked AFTER
+		# ballistic, a deliberate deviation from the canonical order. It only
+		# shifts which synthetic slots pick which clip; the per-slot work this
+		# bench measures is identical either way (CR #597).
+		var intent := INTENT_LOCOMOTION
+		if (st & LIVE_BIT) == 0:
+			intent = INTENT_DEATH
+		elif (st & BALLISTIC_BIT) != 0:
+			intent = INTENT_BALLISTIC
+		elif hitstop > 0:
+			intent = INTENT_STAGGER
+		elif (st & STUCK_BIT) != 0:
+			intent = INTENT_IDLE
+		var clip := float(intent)
+
+		# --- bitcast pack: CUSTOM_DATA.w = flags|intent|seg via floatBitsToUint round-trip ---
+		var flags := st & (LIVE_BIT | BALLISTIC_BIT | STUCK_BIT | HORDE_STATE_MASK)
+		var seg := 0  # reserved gore segment mask (0 in PR3)
+		var packed := (flags & 0xFF) | ((intent & 0xFF) << 8) | ((seg & 0xFFFF) << 16)
+		_scratch.encode_u32(0, packed)
+		var w := _scratch.decode_float(0)
+
+		# --- transform build (basis from heading; sim (X,Y) -> Godot (X,Z)) ---
+		var heading := atan2(bxy, bxx)
+		var b := Basis(Vector3.UP, -heading)
+		var po := live * OUT_STRIDE
+		_out[po + 0] = b.x.x
+		_out[po + 1] = b.y.x
+		_out[po + 2] = b.z.x
+		_out[po + 3] = ox
+		_out[po + 4] = b.x.y
+		_out[po + 5] = b.y.y
+		_out[po + 6] = b.z.y
+		_out[po + 7] = 0.19
+		_out[po + 8] = b.x.z
+		_out[po + 9] = b.y.z
+		_out[po + 10] = b.z.z
+		_out[po + 11] = oy
+		# CUSTOM_DATA v1: x=clock y=clip z=health_frac w=packed-flags
+		_out[po + 12] = clock_base + float(s) * 0.013
+		_out[po + 13] = clip
+		_out[po + 14] = health_frac
+		_out[po + 15] = w
+		live += 1
+	return live
+
+
+# Synthetic but representative inputs: all-live dense horde (worst case for asm),
+# varied heading/state/health so the per-slot branches actually diverge.
+func _seed(n: int) -> void:
+	_agent.resize(n * AGENT_STRIDE)
+	_state.resize(n)
+	_health.resize(n)
+	_hitstop.resize(n)
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+	for i in n:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * 2.0
+		var gy := float(i / side) * 2.0
+		var ang := float(i) * 0.7
+		var cs := cos(ang)
+		var sn := sin(ang)
+		var o := i * AGENT_STRIDE
+		_agent[o + 0] = cs
+		_agent[o + 1] = -sn
+		_agent[o + 2] = 0.0
+		_agent[o + 3] = gx
+		_agent[o + 4] = sn
+		_agent[o + 5] = cs
+		_agent[o + 6] = 0.0
+		_agent[o + 7] = gy
+		# state: mostly live-locomotion, a sprinkling of ballistic/stuck/dead
+		var st := LIVE_BIT | (i % 3)  # HordeState 0..2 in low bits
+		if i % 11 == 0:
+			st |= BALLISTIC_BIT
+		if i % 7 == 0:
+			st |= STUCK_BIT
+		if i % 23 == 0:
+			st &= ~LIVE_BIT  # a few dead -> death intent (still rendered this frame)
+		_state[i] = st
+		_health[i] = (i * 37) % 256
+		_hitstop[i] = 3 if (i % 13 == 0) else 0

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/VatBitpackProbe.gd b/apps/game-godot/VatBitpackProbe.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..b2c9e55
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/VatBitpackProbe.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,221 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR3 — SPIKE 1: floatBitsToUint round-trip on INSTANCE_CUSTOM.
+#
+# Gates the CUSTOM_DATA.w packing decision (spec .totem/specs/p2b-3d-horde.md,
+# measurement gate 1). Packs a battery of known 32-bit patterns into the w channel
+# of a stride-16 MultiMesh buffer (the SAME storage path the production adapter
+# uses), and a self-checking shader emits GREEN per instance iff floatBitsToUint(w)
+# returns the exact bits (ground truth via a clean uniform, not the channel under
+# test). The CPU reads back one pixel per instance and reports PASS/FAIL per class.
+#
+# WHY it matters: the realistic pack (flags|intent|seg in the low 24 bits, high byte
+# zero) lands in the *denormal / zero-exponent* float range — exactly where a fp16
+# custom-data channel or a flush-to-zero driver would corrupt it. The denormal +
+# bitfield patterns below are the load-bearing cases; NaN/Inf test canonicalisation.
+#
+# Needs a REAL GPU (the shader must rasterise) — headless -> no-op + quit, like the
+# VAT path's headless guard. Run:
+#   <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://vat_bitpack_probe.tscn
+# Prints [bitpack] PASS/FAIL lines + a verdict, saves a capture, then quits.
+
+const SHADER_PATH := "res://shaders/vat_bitpack_probe.gdshader"
+const OUT_STRIDE := 16
+const SPACING := 2.0
+const SETTLE_FRAMES := 12  # let the GPU rasterise before readback
+const CAP_DIR := "user://vat_bitpack_probe/"
+
+# [pattern_u32, label, class] — class groups the verdict (finite / denormal / inf / nan).
+const PATTERNS: Array = [
+	[0x00000000, "zero", "finite"],
+	[0x80000000, "neg_zero", "finite"],
+	[0x3F800000, "one_1.0", "finite"],
+	[0xBF800000, "neg_one", "finite"],
+	[0x40490FDB, "pi_low_mantissa", "finite"],
+	[0xDEADBEEF, "arb_DEADBEEF", "finite"],
+	[0x12345678, "arb_12345678", "finite"],
+	[0xAAAAAAAA, "alt_AAAA", "finite"],
+	[0x55555555, "alt_5555", "finite"],
+	[0x3F800003, "normal_low_bits", "finite"],
+	[0x7F7FFFFF, "max_normal", "finite"],
+	[0x00800000, "min_normal", "finite"],
+	[0x00000001, "min_denormal", "denormal"],
+	[0x007FFFFF, "max_denormal", "denormal"],
+	[0x0000FFFF, "low16_denormal", "denormal"],
+	[0x0003A5C7, "pack_clip_flags_seg", "denormal"],  # realistic w-pack (low 24 bits)
+	[0x00A50003, "pack_alt", "denormal"],  # realistic w-pack
+	[0x00FF00FF, "pack_bytes", "denormal"],  # realistic w-pack
+	[0x7F800000, "pos_inf", "inf"],
+	[0xFF800000, "neg_inf", "inf"],
+	[0x7FC00000, "qNaN", "nan"],
+	[0x7FA00000, "sNaN", "nan"],
+	[0xFFFFFFFF, "all_ones", "nan"],
+]
+
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _mmi: MultiMeshInstance3D
+var _frame := 0
+var _done := false
+var _scratch := PackedByteArray()
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	_scratch.resize(4)
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		print("[bitpack] headless — cannot rasterise the shader; no-op + quit")
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+
+	var n := PATTERNS.size()
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+
+	# camera: orthographic, on +Z looking at the XY grid (quads face +Z -> full-on)
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_ORTHOGONAL
+	var extent := float(side) * SPACING
+	_cam.size = extent + SPACING
+	var cx := float(side - 1) * SPACING * 0.5
+	var cy := -float(int(ceil(float(n) / float(side))) - 1) * SPACING * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(cx, cy, 20.0)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(cx, cy, 0.0), Vector3.UP)
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+
+	# MultiMesh — SAME storage path as the production adapter (TRANSFORM_3D + custom data)
+	var mm := MultiMesh.new()
+	mm.transform_format = MultiMesh.TRANSFORM_3D
+	mm.use_custom_data = true
+	var quad := QuadMesh.new()
+	quad.size = Vector2(1.6, 1.6)
+	mm.mesh = quad
+
+	var mat := ShaderMaterial.new()
+	mat.shader = load(SHADER_PATH) as Shader
+
+	# ground truth: each u32 as its two's-complement int32 (clean uniform, full int32)
+	var expected := PackedInt32Array()
+	expected.resize(32)  # matches the shader's expected[32]; padding never indexed
+	for i in n:
+		var p: int = PATTERNS[i][0]
+		expected[i] = p if p < 0x80000000 else p - 0x100000000
+	mat.set_shader_parameter("expected", expected)
+
+	# buffer: 16 floats/instance; transform = identity at grid pos; w = bitcast(pattern)
+	var buf := PackedFloat32Array()
+	buf.resize(n * OUT_STRIDE)
+	for i in n:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * SPACING
+		var gy := -float(i / side) * SPACING
+		var po := i * OUT_STRIDE
+		# identity basis, translation (gx, gy, 0)
+		buf[po + 0] = 1.0
+		buf[po + 3] = gx
+		buf[po + 5] = 1.0
+		buf[po + 7] = gy
+		buf[po + 10] = 1.0
+		buf[po + 11] = 0.0
+		# CUSTOM_DATA: x/y/z unused, w = the bit pattern under test
+		buf[po + 15] = _bitcast(PATTERNS[i][0])
+	mm.instance_count = n
+	mm.buffer = buf
+
+	_mmi = MultiMeshInstance3D.new()
+	_mmi.multimesh = mm
+	_mmi.material_override = mat
+	add_child(_mmi)
+
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(CAP_DIR))
+	print("[bitpack] start; patterns=", n)
+
+
+func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
+	if _done:
+		return
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < SETTLE_FRAMES:
+		return
+	_done = true
+	_verify()
+	get_tree().quit(0)
+
+
+func _verify() -> void:
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img == null:
+		print("[bitpack] FAIL: no viewport image (cannot read back)")
+		return
+	var w := img.get_width()
+	var h := img.get_height()
+	var n := PATTERNS.size()
+	var fail_by_class := {"finite": 0, "denormal": 0, "inf": 0, "nan": 0}
+	var total_by_class := {"finite": 0, "denormal": 0, "inf": 0, "nan": 0}
+	var fails := 0
+
+	for i in n:
+		var p: int = PATTERNS[i][0]
+		var label: String = PATTERNS[i][1]
+		var cls: String = PATTERNS[i][2]
+		total_by_class[cls] += 1
+		# grid pos exactly as built in _ready()
+		var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+		var world := Vector3(float(i % side) * SPACING, -float(i / side) * SPACING, 0.0)
+		var sp := _cam.unproject_position(world)
+		var ok := _sample_pass(img, w, h, sp)
+		if not ok:
+			fails += 1
+			fail_by_class[cls] += 1
+		print("[bitpack] %s 0x%08X %s (%s)" % ["PASS" if ok else "FAIL", p, label, cls])
+
+	var shot := CAP_DIR + "bitpack.png"
+	img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(shot))
+	print("[bitpack] capture %s" % shot)
+
+	print(
+		(
+			"[bitpack] by-class fails: finite=%d/%d denormal=%d/%d inf=%d/%d nan=%d/%d"
+			% [
+				fail_by_class["finite"],
+				total_by_class["finite"],
+				fail_by_class["denormal"],
+				total_by_class["denormal"],
+				fail_by_class["inf"],
+				total_by_class["inf"],
+				fail_by_class["nan"],
+				total_by_class["nan"],
+			]
+		)
+	)
+	# VERDICT: the production w-pack lives in the finite + denormal classes.
+	var pack_ok: bool = fail_by_class["finite"] == 0 and fail_by_class["denormal"] == 0
+	if fails == 0:
+		print("[bitpack] VERDICT: GO — full 32-bit round-trip exact (all classes incl. NaN/Inf)")
+	elif pack_ok:
+		print(
+			"[bitpack] VERDICT: GO (constrained) — finite+denormal exact; only NaN/Inf differ (pack avoids those)"
+		)
+	else:
+		print(
+			"[bitpack] VERDICT: NO-GO — finite/denormal corruption; w falls back to flags-only finite-normal subset"
+		)
+
+
+# argmax(green vs red) over a 3x3 neighbourhood — robust to sRGB/tonemap.
+func _sample_pass(img: Image, w: int, h: int, sp: Vector2) -> bool:
+	var votes := 0
+	var samples := 0
+	for dy in range(-1, 2):
+		for dx in range(-1, 2):
+			var x := int(round(sp.x)) + dx
+			var y := int(round(sp.y)) + dy
+			if x < 0 or y < 0 or x >= w or y >= h:
+				continue
+			var c := img.get_pixel(x, y)
+			samples += 1
+			if c.g > c.r:
+				votes += 1
+	return samples > 0 and votes * 2 > samples
+
+
+func _bitcast(u: int) -> float:
+	_scratch.encode_u32(0, u)
+	return _scratch.decode_float(0)

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/horde_render_bench.gd b/apps/game-godot/horde_render_bench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..bbd016c
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/horde_render_bench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,218 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR3 — production HordeRenderAdapter verification bench.
+#
+# Drives the production adapter from a SYNTHETIC stride-8 transform buffer + a
+# SYNTHETIC stride-4 render-state buffer (the get_agent_render_state_buffer()
+# FFI shape), exercising the NEW behaviors the disposable proof never did:
+#   - intent → clip selection (varied AnimIntent per slot),
+#   - HitStop clock-freeze (a slot with hitstop > 0 holds while the rest run),
+#   - health tint (z channel) + the packed-flags w channel (ballistic accent),
+#   - dead-slot masking (zeroed transform → not drawn).
+#
+# Asserts the CPU-side logic programmatically (clip table + clock freeze +
+# live count), then captures a frame for eyeballing. Needs a real GPU — run:
+#   <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://horde_render_bench.tscn
+# Prints [horde-bench] PASS/FAIL lines + a verdict, then quits.
+
+const N := 64
+const SPACING := 2.0
+const DELTA := 0.1
+const FRAMES := 4  # advance the clock a few frames before asserting
+const CAP_DIR := "user://horde_render_bench/"
+
+# mirror of HordeRenderAdapter's AnimIntent / render-state layout
+const ANIM_IDLE := 0
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_STAGGER := 2
+const ANIM_BALLISTIC := 3
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const BALLISTIC_BIT := 0x10
+
+var _adapter: HordeRenderAdapter
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _xf := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _rs := PackedByteArray()
+var _frame := 0
+var _done := false
+# slots reserved for the hitstop-freeze assertion
+var _frozen_slot := 5
+var _running_slot := 6
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		print("[horde-bench] headless — cannot rasterise; no-op + quit")
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+	_cam.fov = 55.0
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+	_frame_camera(N)
+
+	_adapter = HordeRenderAdapter.new(self, false)
+	if not _adapter.setup():
+		print("[horde-bench] FAIL: adapter setup returned false")
+		get_tree().quit(1)
+		return
+	_adapter.set_active(true)
+	_build_buffers()
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(CAP_DIR))
+	print(
+		(
+			"[horde-bench] start; N=%d clips=%s"
+			% [N, str(_adapter._meta.get("clips", []).map(func(c): return c.get("name", "?")))]
+		)
+	)
+
+
+func _frame_camera(n: int) -> void:
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(n))))
+	var extent := float(side) * SPACING
+	var c := extent * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(c, extent * 0.95, extent * 1.45)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(c, 0.5, c), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+func _build_buffers() -> void:
+	_xf.resize(N * 8)
+	_rs.resize(N * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	var side := int(ceil(sqrt(float(N))))
+	for i in N:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * SPACING
+		var gy := float(i / side) * SPACING
+		var ang := float(i) * 0.7
+		var cs := cos(ang)
+		var sn := sin(ang)
+		var o := i * 8
+		# Transform2D: [bx.x, by.x, 0, ox, bx.y, by.y, 0, oy]
+		_xf[o + 0] = cs
+		_xf[o + 1] = -sn
+		_xf[o + 2] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 3] = gx
+		_xf[o + 4] = sn
+		_xf[o + 5] = cs
+		_xf[o + 6] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 7] = gy
+		# render-state record
+		var intent := i % 5  # cycle Idle/Locomotion/Stagger/Ballistic/Death
+		var health := (i * 37) % 256
+		var hitstop := 0
+		if i == _frozen_slot:
+			hitstop = 6  # this slot must NOT advance its clock
+			intent = ANIM_LOCOMOTION
+		if i == _running_slot:
+			intent = ANIM_LOCOMOTION  # control: must advance
+		# Compute flags from the FINAL intent, mirroring the production ABI:
+		# LIVE_BIT is clear for Death (CR #597) so the synthetic dataset never
+		# ships a contradictory LIVE_BIT + ANIM_DEATH record.
+		var flags := 0
+		if intent != ANIM_DEATH:
+			flags |= LIVE_BIT
+		if intent == ANIM_BALLISTIC:
+			flags |= BALLISTIC_BIT
+		var ro := i * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		_rs[ro + 0] = flags
+		_rs[ro + 1] = health
+		_rs[ro + 2] = hitstop
+		_rs[ro + 3] = intent
+	# slot 0 = a dead slot (zeroed transform) → must be masked out of the draw
+	for k in 8:
+		_xf[k] = 0.0
+
+
+func _process(_delta: float) -> void:
+	if _done:
+		return
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, N, DELTA)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < FRAMES:
+		return
+	_done = true
+	var fails := _verify()
+	# Exit non-zero on any assertion failure so the bench can gate automation
+	# instead of always reporting success (CR #597).
+	get_tree().quit(0 if fails == 0 else 1)
+
+
+func _verify() -> int:
+	var fails := 0
+
+	# 1. intent → clip table well-formed + differentiates when ≥2 named clips.
+	var clips: Array = _adapter._meta.get("clips", [])
+	var clip_count := clips.size()
+	var loco_clip: int = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION]
+	var death_clip: int = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH]
+	if loco_clip < 0 or loco_clip >= clip_count or death_clip < 0 or death_clip >= clip_count:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			(
+				"[horde-bench] FAIL: clip out of range (Locomotion=%d, Death=%d, valid=0..%d)"
+				% [loco_clip, death_clip, clip_count - 1]
+			)
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: Locomotion → clip %d, Death → clip %d" % [loco_clip, death_clip])
+	if clip_count >= 2 and loco_clip == death_clip:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			"[horde-bench] FAIL: Locomotion and Death map to the same clip despite ≥2 named clips"
+		)
+	elif clip_count >= 2:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: intents differentiate (Locomotion ≠ Death clip)")
+
+	# 2. HitStop clock-freeze: the frozen slot held; the control advanced.
+	var frozen_clock: float = _adapter._clock[_frozen_slot]
+	var running_clock: float = _adapter._clock[_running_slot]
+	if frozen_clock != 0.0:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			"[horde-bench] FAIL: hitstop slot clock advanced to %.3f (must stay 0)" % frozen_clock
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: hitstop slot clock frozen at 0")
+	var expected_running := float(FRAMES) * DELTA
+	if absf(running_clock - expected_running) > 1e-4:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			(
+				"[horde-bench] FAIL: running slot clock %.3f, expected %.3f"
+				% [running_clock, expected_running]
+			)
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: running slot clock advanced to %.3f" % running_clock)
+
+	# 3. dead-slot masking: slot 0 zeroed → live count is N-1.
+	if _adapter.last_live != N - 1:
+		fails += 1
+		print(
+			(
+				"[horde-bench] FAIL: live=%d, expected %d (one dead slot masked)"
+				% [_adapter.last_live, N - 1]
+			)
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] PASS: dead slot masked (live=%d)" % _adapter.last_live)
+
+	# capture for eyeballing (health gradient + ballistic accents + animation)
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := CAP_DIR + "horde_render.png"
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print("[horde-bench] capture %s" % path)
+
+	if fails == 0:
+		print(
+			(
+				"[horde-bench] VERDICT: GO — production adapter logic verified (asm %.3fms @ %d)"
+				% [_adapter.last_assembly_usec / 1000.0, _adapter.last_live]
+			)
+		)
+	else:
+		print("[horde-bench] VERDICT: FAIL — %d assertion(s) failed" % fails)
+	return fails

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/shaders/horde_render.gdshader b/apps/game-godot/shaders/horde_render.gdshader
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..0c5e26a
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/shaders/horde_render.gdshader
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+shader_type spatial;
+render_mode unshaded;
+
+// ADR-029 P2b PR3 — production VAT horde shader (CUSTOM_DATA v1, the stable
+// P2b ABI). Successor to the disposable proof shader `vat_horde.gdshader`.
+//
+// Vertex positions come ENTIRELY from the baked float VAT texture (UV2.x =
+// the original vertex index as (index+0.5)/N — survives Godot vertex
+// splitting; row = animation frame from a PER-INSTANCE clock). There is NO
+// global TIME, so HitStop is the CPU-side adapter declining to advance a
+// slot's clock while the rest animate.
+//
+// INSTANCE_CUSTOM v1 (proven bit-exact through the channel by the
+// floatBitsToUint round-trip spike — finite + denormal classes all exact):
+//   x = anim clock (seconds, CPU-owned, stoppable for HitStop)
+//   y = clip index (numeric, from the bake clip table)
+//   z = health fraction [0,1] (unquantized — gore/dissolve math wants it raw)
+//   w = packed flags uint: floatBitsToUint(w) yields the Rust render-state
+//       flags byte (LIVE / BALLISTIC + reserved gore mask). The realistic
+//       pack lives in the denormal range, which the spike proved exact.
+
+uniform sampler2D vat_tex : filter_nearest, repeat_disable;
+uniform int total_rows = 1;
+uniform float fps = 24.0;
+uniform int clip_count = 1;
+uniform float clip_row_start[8];
+uniform float clip_frame_count[8];
+uniform vec3 base_color : source_color = vec3(0.30, 0.55, 0.30);
+uniform vec3 hurt_color : source_color = vec3(1.0, 0.05, 0.05);
+uniform vec3 ballistic_color : source_color = vec3(0.86, 0.66, 0.22);
+uniform bool use_colormap = false; // OFF = flat tint (clean perf path)
+uniform sampler2D colormap : filter_nearest, source_color; // Kenney palette (UV lookup)
+// ADR-029 P2b PR3: the dmg-based red health tint is OFF by default so the clean
+// Kenney textures show for the 3D-artist preview; flip on to re-enable health viz.
+uniform bool use_health_tint = false;
+
+// Flag bits — mirror of the Rust render-state flags byte (density_feedback.rs
+// BALLISTIC_BIT). Decoded from the packed `w` channel to prove the
+// floatBitsToUint ABI end-to-end; the gore mask (high bytes) is reserved.
+const uint BALLISTIC_BIT = 0x10u;
+
+varying float v_health;
+varying flat float v_ballistic;
+
+void vertex() {
+	// Self-safe clip index: setup() guards clip_count into [1, 8], but the
+	// shader must not trust the uniform — clamp into [0, 7] so neither a 0
+	// (underflow to -1) nor a clip_count/index > 8 can read past the
+	// 8-element clip_row_start / clip_frame_count uniforms (CR #597).
+	int max_clip = max(min(clip_count, 8) - 1, 0);
+	int clip = clamp(int(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.y + 0.5), 0, max_clip);
+	float cstart = clip_row_start[clip];
+	float ccount = max(clip_frame_count[clip], 1.0);
+	float dur = ccount / max(fps, 0.001); // guard div-by-zero on the fps uniform
+	float local = fract(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.x / dur); // [0,1) looped per-instance clock
+	float frame = min(floor(local * ccount), ccount - 1.0);
+	float v = (cstart + frame + 0.5) / float(total_rows);
+	VERTEX = textureLod(vat_tex, vec2(UV2.x, v), 0.0).xyz;
+	v_health = clamp(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.z, 0.0, 1.0);
+	uint flags = floatBitsToUint(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.w); // proven bit-exact (spike 1)
+	v_ballistic = ((flags & BALLISTIC_BIT) != 0u) ? 1.0 : 0.0;
+}
+
+void fragment() {
+	vec3 body = use_colormap ? texture(colormap, UV).rgb : base_color;
+	// Health tint: lerp toward hurt as health drops (z = health fraction). Gated
+	// OFF by default (use_health_tint) — clean Kenney textures for the preview.
+	if (use_health_tint) {
+		// Squared curve so damage reads EARLY (ADR-029 P2b PR3 showcase): a single
+		// pistol hit (70% health) already shows clearly red — `v_health*v_health`
+		// maps 0.7 → 0.49 (≈half red) instead of the faint 0.7 a linear lerp gives.
+		body = mix(hurt_color, body, v_health * v_health);
+	}
+	// Ballistic accent, decoded from the packed flags uint (exercises the
+	// CUSTOM_DATA.w ABI; gore-driven dissolve lands on the reserved mask).
+	body = mix(body, ballistic_color, 0.5 * v_ballistic);
+	ALBEDO = body;
+}

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/shaders/vat_bitpack_probe.gdshader b/apps/game-godot/shaders/vat_bitpack_probe.gdshader
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..04d291b
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/shaders/vat_bitpack_probe.gdshader
@@ -0,0 +1,31 @@
+shader_type spatial;
+render_mode unshaded;
+
+// ADR-029 P2b PR3 — SPIKE 1: floatBitsToUint round-trip on INSTANCE_CUSTOM.
+//
+// Gates CUSTOM_DATA.w packing: can a full 32-bit uint survive the
+// GDScript -> MultiMesh stride-16 buffer -> vertex shader -> floatBitsToUint()
+// path BIT-EXACT in Godot 4.6 (i.e. is the per-instance custom-data channel real
+// fp32, not fp16 / NaN-canonicalised / denormal-flushed)? If yes, w can carry a
+// packed uint; if not, w falls back to a flags-only finite-float subset.
+//
+// Self-checking: the EXPECTED uint arrives via a clean `expected[]` uniform (full
+// int32, NOT through the custom-data channel under test). The shader decodes the
+// channel-under-test with floatBitsToUint and emits GREEN iff the bits match the
+// ground truth, RED otherwise. CPU reads back one pixel per instance and classifies
+// by argmax(channel) — robust to sRGB/tonemap (a primary green stays > red).
+
+const int MAX_PATTERNS = 32;
+uniform int expected[MAX_PATTERNS]; // ground truth, two's-complement reinterpret of the u32
+
+varying flat float v_ok;
+
+void vertex() {
+	uint got = floatBitsToUint(INSTANCE_CUSTOM.w);      // the channel under test
+	uint want = uint(expected[INSTANCE_ID]);            // int->uint = bit reinterpret (GLSL)
+	v_ok = (got == want) ? 1.0 : 0.0;
+}
+
+void fragment() {
+	ALBEDO = (v_ok > 0.5) ? vec3(0.0, 1.0, 0.0) : vec3(1.0, 0.0, 0.0);
+}

+/// Semantic animation intent for an agent's render-LOD-0 / VAT clip
+/// selection (ADR-029 P2b). NET-NEW: the sim has no animation-state enum
+/// today — [`HordeState`] is *density* classification, not animation.
+///
+/// The sim publishes *intent* (what the agent is doing), NOT a clip id or
+/// a raw velocity; the Godot render adapter maps intent → clip name →
+/// numeric clip index from the bake metadata (the render-ownership split,
+/// ADR-029 fold). A pure function of the agent's components, so the byte is
+/// determinism-clean (ADR-013): same seed + input stream → same intent.
+///
+/// `#[repr(u8)]` values are the wire contract the GDScript adapter mirrors
+/// (the mirror + its parity test land with the production adapter in the
+/// next P2b slice). `Attack` is RESERVED — published only when the sim
+/// grows an attack component to drive it (none exists in P2b PR3), per the
+/// cohort fold "the attack clip lands only if the sim has an attack intent
+/// to publish."
+#[derive(Copy, Clone, Debug, Default, Eq, PartialEq)]
+#[repr(u8)]
+pub enum AnimIntent {
+    /// Live, at rest — per-tick translation below the idle cutoff.
+    #[default]
+    Idle = 0,
+    /// Live, moving under its own locomotion.
+    Locomotion = 1,
+    /// Poise-broken: carries [`Staggered`].
+    Stagger = 2,
+    /// In-flight: carries [`BallisticMode`] (catapulted / tossed / launched).
+    Ballistic = 3,
+    /// Dying/dead this frame ([`DeadTag`] or `Health::is_dead`). Published
+    /// for ABI completeness; true death PLAYBACK is deferred (the snapshot
+    /// zeroes despawned slots, so render-death needs the linger /
+    /// last-transform contract — an ADR-029 P2b non-goal). The adapter
+    /// holds-pose / idles on Death until that contract lands.
+    Death = 4,
+    /// RESERVED — published only once an attack component exists to drive it.
+    Attack = 5,
+}
+
+/// Per-slot record stride for [`AgentRenderStateBuffer`] (ADR-029 P2b PR3,
+/// Surface A). Bytes per slot:
+///   `[0]` flags  — bit 2 [`LIVE_BIT`] + bit 4 [`BALLISTIC_BIT`] (reused from
+///                  the horde-state bit protocol). Bits 0/1/7 are unused in
+///                  render-state records today; bits 3/5/6 are reserved for
+///                  coarse gore flags.
+///   `[1]` health — [`quantize_health_fraction`] (255 = full, 0 = dead).
+///   `[2]` hitstop— [`HitStop::remaining_frames`] (0 = none); the render
+///                  adapter freezes that instance's anim clock while > 0.
+///   `[3]` intent — [`AnimIntent`] discriminant → clip (Godot-side map).
+///
+/// Surface A of the P2b ABI: the wide CPU-authoritative render state,
+/// consumed render-side to assemble `CUSTOM_DATA` — NOT uploaded to the GPU
+/// as a lookup texture. It supersedes the production path's need to read
+/// `get_agent_horde_states` + `get_agent_health_buffer` separately.
+/// Extending the record (e.g. a gore-segment byte) is a coordinated
+/// Rust+GDScript stride bump guarded by the buffer's parity test.
+pub const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE: usize = 4;
+
+/// `[0]` flags byte offset within a [`RENDER_STATE_STRIDE`] record.
+pub const RS_FLAGS: usize = 0;
+/// `[1]` health-fraction byte offset.
+pub const RS_HEALTH: usize = 1;
+/// `[2]` hit-stop-remaining-frames byte offset.
+pub const RS_HITSTOP: usize = 2;
+/// `[3]` [`AnimIntent`] discriminant byte offset.
+pub const RS_INTENT: usize = 3;
+
+/// Per-agent render-state FFI buffer (ADR-029 P2b PR3 — Surface A).
+///
+/// Mirrors [`AgentHordeStateBuffer`] / [`AgentHealthBuffer`] but carries a
+/// [`RENDER_STATE_STRIDE`]-byte record per slot (flags / health / hitstop /
+/// intent), indexed by [`BufferSlot`] so slot `i`'s record lives at
+/// `buf[i * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE ..]` and lines up with the agent whose
+/// transform is at `snapshot[i * SNAPSHOT_STRIDE]`. Written each tick by
+/// [`agent_render_state_publish_system`]; read by
+/// `ZomboidSim::get_agent_render_state_buffer` via `try_read` so a contended
+/// or poisoned lock never panics the Godot main thread. Pure function of
+/// components → determinism-clean (ADR-013).
+///
+/// Length parity with the snapshot slot count (× the stride) is locked by
+/// `agent_render_state_buffer_length_matches_snapshot_slot_count`.
+#[derive(Resource, Clone, Default)]
+pub struct AgentRenderStateBuffer(pub Arc<RwLock<Vec<u8>>>);
+
+impl AgentRenderStateBuffer {
+    pub fn new() -> Self {
+        Self::default()
+    }
+
+    pub fn handle(&self) -> Arc<RwLock<Vec<u8>>> {
+        Arc::clone(&self.0)
+    }
+}

 /// Quantize a `Health` component to the 0–255 fraction byte the FFI ships.

 /// `round((current / max) * 255)` clamped to `[0, 255]`. Pure function —

+/// Derive the semantic [`AnimIntent`] for one agent. Pure function of the
+/// agent's components — no RNG, no wall-clock, no cross-agent state — so the
+/// published byte is determinism-clean (ADR-013) and each branch of the
+/// priority ladder is trivially unit-testable.
+///
+/// Priority (first match wins): **Death > Stagger > Ballistic > Idle /
+/// Locomotion**. HitStop is deliberately NOT an intent — it freezes the
+/// *current* clip and ships in its own record byte, so a hit-stopped walking
+/// agent keeps `Locomotion` intent (the adapter just stops advancing its
+/// clock). The idle cutoff reuses the diagnostic stuck velocity-magnitude
+/// threshold ([`STUCK_VEL_MAG_SQ_THRESHOLD`]) as the PR3 starting heuristic;
+/// it can graduate to its own tunable if the idle band wants to differ from
+/// the stuck-diagnostic band.
+#[inline]
+pub fn derive_anim_intent(
+    health: Option<&Health>,
+    staggered: Option<&Staggered>,
+    ballistic: Option<&BallisticMode>,
+    dead: bool,
+    velocity: &Velocity,
+) -> AnimIntent {
+    if dead || health.is_some_and(|h| h.is_dead()) {
+        AnimIntent::Death
+    } else if staggered.is_some() {
+        AnimIntent::Stagger
+    } else if ballistic.is_some() {
+        AnimIntent::Ballistic
+    } else {
+        let mag_sq = velocity.dx * velocity.dx + velocity.dy * velocity.dy;
+        if mag_sq.is_finite() && mag_sq < STUCK_VEL_MAG_SQ_THRESHOLD {
+            AnimIntent::Idle
+        } else {
+            AnimIntent::Locomotion
+        }
+    }
+}
+
+/// Publish the per-agent render-state record buffer parallel to the snapshot
+/// (ADR-029 P2b PR3 — Surface A). Sibling of [`agent_health_publish_system`]
+/// / [`agent_horde_state_publish_system`]: pure component → bytes, indexed
+/// by [`BufferSlot`], FFI-only (no in-sim consumer). Sizing semantics mirror
+/// the siblings — re-use the allocation when the slot count is stable;
+/// resize + unconditional `fill(0)` so a despawned higher slot can't leave
+/// stale bytes in a reused lower record.
+///
+/// Schedule position (`systems.rs`): `.after(snapshot_write_system)
+/// .before(hit_stop_decay_system)` — the SAME pre-decay window the snapshot
+/// occupies, so the published `HitStop` / `Staggered` reflect the active
+/// frame Godot consumes (the decay systems run the tick after), and the
+/// read/write order against every component writer is fully pinned.
+///
+/// Determinism (ADR-013): each slot is written independently from a pure
+/// function of that agent's components, so archetype iteration order cannot
+/// perturb the output.
+// Clippy's `type_complexity` flags the 7-element Query tuple. Same rationale
+// as `vehicle_input_arbitration_system` / `damage_resolution_system`: a
+// dedicated `QueryData` derive isn't worth it at this slice's scope; the
+// annotation is targeted.
+#[allow(clippy::type_complexity)]
+pub fn agent_render_state_publish_system(
+    buffer: Res<AgentRenderStateBuffer>,
+    q: Query<(
+        &BufferSlot,
+        &Velocity,
+        Option<&Health>,
+        Option<&HitStop>,
+        Option<&Staggered>,
+        Option<&BallisticMode>,
+        Option<&DeadTag>,
+    )>,
+) {
+    // max_slot before the write guard so the O(N) scan stays out of the lock
+    // window (GCA R1 finding on `snapshot_write_system`, PR #87).
+    let max_slot = q.iter().map(|(slot, ..)| slot.0).max();
+    let Ok(mut buf) = buffer.0.write() else {
+        return;
+    };
+    let Some(max_slot) = max_slot else {
+        buf.clear();
+        return;
+    };
+    let required_len = (max_slot as usize + 1) * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE;
+    if buf.len() != required_len {
+        buf.resize(required_len, 0);
+    }
+    buf.fill(0);
+    for (slot, velocity, health, hitstop, staggered, ballistic, dead) in q.iter() {
+        let base = slot.0 as usize * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE;
+        let intent = derive_anim_intent(health, staggered, ballistic, dead.is_some(), velocity);
+        // LIVE_BIT mirrors the horde-state bit protocol: it must be CLEAR for a
+        // dead agent (`DeadTag` or `Health::is_dead` — exactly the predicate
+        // `derive_anim_intent` collapses into `AnimIntent::Death`), so the flags
+        // byte never contradicts the intent byte it ships alongside. A dead
+        // ballistic corpse is therefore `BALLISTIC_BIT` alone, not
+        // `LIVE_BIT | BALLISTIC_BIT`. No current consumer masks on LIVE_BIT, but
+        // the ABI is documented to be extended with gore-segment flags — a
+        // future `flags & LIVE_BIT == 0` skip must not read a dying corpse as
+        // live (Greptile P1 / GCA, #597).
+        let mut flags = if intent == AnimIntent::Death {
+            0
+        } else {
+            LIVE_BIT
+        };
+        if ballistic.is_some() {
+            flags |= BALLISTIC_BIT;
+        }
+        // Death always publishes health 0 so the record's fields never
+        // contradict each other — a `DeadTag` agent with no `Health` would
+        // otherwise ship intent=Death + flags=0 + health=255 (CR #597). Live
+        // agents missing `Health` → full (255), matching
+        // `get_agent_health_tint_buffer`'s "absent health = full/green" fallback.
+        let health_byte = if intent == AnimIntent::Death {
+            0
+        } else {
+            health.map_or(255, quantize_health_fraction)
+        };
+        let hitstop_frames = hitstop.map_or(0, |h| h.remaining_frames);
+        buf[base + RS_FLAGS] = flags;
+        buf[base + RS_HEALTH] = health_byte;
+        buf[base + RS_HITSTOP] = hitstop_frames;
+        buf[base + RS_INTENT] = intent as u8;
+    }
+}

+    fn run_render_state_publish(world: &mut World) {
+        let mut sys = IntoSystem::into_system(agent_render_state_publish_system);
+        sys.initialize(world);
+        let _ = sys.run((), world);

+    #[test]
+    fn derive_anim_intent_priority_ladder() {
+        let moving = Velocity {
+            dx: 5.0,
+            dy: 0.0,
+            dz: 0.0,
+        };
+        let still = Velocity {
+            dx: 0.0,
+            dy: 0.0,
+            dz: 0.0,
+        };
+        let alive = Health {
+            current: 100.0,
+            max: 100.0,
+        };
+        let dead = Health {
+            current: 0.0,
+            max: 100.0,
+        };
+        let stag = Staggered {
+            remaining_duration: 0.2,
+        };
+        let ball = BallisticMode {
+            velocity: [1.0, 0.0, 2.0],
+            spawned_at_tick: 0,
+        };
+
+        // Death dominates everything (even with stagger + ballistic present).
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), Some(&stag), Some(&ball), true, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Death
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&dead), None, None, false, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Death,
+            "is_dead health → Death even without DeadTag"
+        );
+        // Stagger over ballistic + locomotion.
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), Some(&stag), Some(&ball), false, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Stagger
+        );
+        // Ballistic over locomotion.
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), None, Some(&ball), false, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Ballistic
+        );
+        // Moving live agent → Locomotion; still agent → Idle.
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), None, None, false, &moving),
+            AnimIntent::Locomotion
+        );
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(Some(&alive), None, None, false, &still),
+            AnimIntent::Idle
+        );
+        // Missing Health still classifies on motion (the publish system
+        // supplies the default health byte, not the derive).
+        assert_eq!(
+            derive_anim_intent(None, None, None, false, &still),
+            AnimIntent::Idle
+        );
+    }
+

+        assert_eq!(buf[b1 + RS_HITSTOP], 6);

+        let b2 = 2 * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE;

+        assert_eq!(buf[b2 + RS_HEALTH], 204, "round(0.8 * 255) = 204");

+        // byte must agree with the `AnimIntent::Death` it ships, or a future
+        // `flags & LIVE_BIT` consumer reads a corpse as live (Greptile P1 /
+        // GCA, #597). Note slot 2 of `render_state_publish_writes_record_per_slot`

+        // this test pins the dead side of that boundary.

diff --git a/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/anim_intent_parity.rs b/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/anim_intent_parity.rs
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..ceffb39
--- /dev/null
+++ b/packages/zomboid-sim/tests/anim_intent_parity.rs
@@ -0,0 +1,108 @@
+//! Cross-language parity for the `AnimIntent` enum (ADR-029 P2b PR3).
+//!
+//! `AnimIntent` is canonical in `density_feedback.rs` as a `#[repr(u8)]` enum.
+//! The Godot production render adapter `HordeRenderAdapter.gd` mirrors the
+//! discriminants as `const ANIM_* := N`. This test reads the GDScript via
+//! `include_str!` and asserts the mirror matches the Rust discriminants,
+//! catching silent desync if a future change moves only one side — the same
+//! discipline `bit_protocol_parity.rs` applies to the horde-state bit protocol.
+//!
+//! Adding/moving an intent:
+//!   1. Edit the `AnimIntent` enum in `density_feedback.rs`.
+//!   2. Mirror the `const ANIM_* := N` in `HordeRenderAdapter.gd`.
+//!   3. Extend the mirror table below.
+
+use zomboid_sim::density_feedback::AnimIntent;
+
+const HORDE_RENDER_ADAPTER_GD: &str =
+    include_str!("../../../apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd");
+
+/// Scan a GDScript file for `const NAME := <decimal>` (or `const NAME: int =
+/// <decimal>`) and return the parsed `u8`. Returns `None` if absent. Matches
+/// at word boundaries so `const ANIM_IDLE` is not matched by a query for a
+/// shorter prefix.
+fn extract_const_int(content: &str, name: &str) -> Option<u8> {
+    let prefix = format!("const {name}");
+    for line in content.lines() {
+        let trimmed = line.trim_start();
+        if !trimmed.starts_with(&prefix) {
+            continue;
+        }
+        // Word-boundary: the char after the name must not extend an identifier
+        // (so `ANIM_DEATH` is not matched by a query for `ANIM_DEAT`, and a
+        // query for `ANIM_IDLE` does not match `const ANIM_IDLE_FOO`).
+        let after_name = &trimmed[prefix.len()..];
+        match after_name.chars().next() {
+            Some(c) if c.is_ascii_alphanumeric() || c == '_' => continue,
+            _ => {}
+        }
+        // Works for both `:=` (GDScript inference) and `: int =` forms — the
+        // value token is the first whitespace-delimited run after the `=`.
+        let Some(eq_pos) = trimmed.find('=') else {
+            continue;
+        };
+        let after_eq = trimmed[eq_pos + 1..].trim_start();
+        let Some(token) = after_eq.split_whitespace().next() else {
+            continue;
+        };
+        let token = token.trim_end_matches(',');
+        if let Ok(val) = token.parse::<u8>() {
+            return Some(val);
+        }
+    }
+    None
+}
+
+struct Mirror {
+    local_name: &'static str,
+    canonical: u8,
+}
+
+#[test]
+fn horde_render_adapter_gd_anim_intent_mirror_matches_rust() {
+    let mirrors = [
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_IDLE",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Idle as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_LOCOMOTION",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Locomotion as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_STAGGER",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Stagger as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_BALLISTIC",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Ballistic as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_DEATH",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Death as u8,
+        },
+        Mirror {
+            local_name: "ANIM_ATTACK",
+            canonical: AnimIntent::Attack as u8,
+        },
+    ];
+    let mut failures = Vec::new();
+    for m in &mirrors {
+        match extract_const_int(HORDE_RENDER_ADAPTER_GD, m.local_name) {
+            Some(v) if v == m.canonical => {}
+            Some(v) => failures.push(format!(
+                "HordeRenderAdapter.gd: `const {}` = {}, expected {} (Rust AnimIntent)",
+                m.local_name, v, m.canonical
+            )),
+            None => failures.push(format!(
+                "HordeRenderAdapter.gd: `const {}` not found (expected {})",
+                m.local_name, m.canonical
+            )),
+        }
+    }
+    assert!(
+        failures.is_empty(),
+        "AnimIntent cross-language parity failures:\n  - {}",
+        failures.join("\n  - ")
+    );
+}
