diff --git a/apps/game-godot/SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd b/apps/game-godot/SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..09ec199
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/SkeletalHordeAdapter.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,421 @@
+class_name SkeletalHordeAdapter
+extends RefCounted
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR4 — skeletal render-LOD-0 adapter (DARK / bench-only this PR).
+#
+# A pool of up to `cap` skinned character-female-a rigs (Skeleton3D +
+# AnimationPlayer), driven from the SAME render-state ABI the VAT horde consumes:
+#   - get_agent_buffer()             stride-8 Transform2D (position + heading)
+#   - get_agent_render_state_buffer() stride-4 [flags, health, hitstop, AnimIntent]
+# The clip set is the rig's OWN AnimationPlayer library (the full Kenney set),
+# resolved AnimIntent -> clip name by preference at setup. NEVER queries the sim
+# FFI itself — the bench (PR4) / sim_driver (PR5) feeds it both buffers.
+#
+# SCOPE (operator ruling 2026-06-22, dark/bench-only): this PR builds + measures
+# the mechanism. WHICH live agents bind here, and the VAT<->skeletal dedup, are
+# PR5's LODManager3D. In-game stays always-VAT until PR5; the forced-N bench is
+# the only consumer this PR. PR4's job: "can we instantiate, bind, animate, tint,
+# freeze, and MEASURE LOD-0 rigs safely?" -> pick the cap from the bench.
+#
+# Cohort impl-preflight folds (lc-codex / lc-agy / lc-gemini, 2026-06-22):
+#  - Shared per-slot clock via SlotClockBook (phase-matched swaps free in PR5).
+#  - Tint via instance uniform on EVERY skinned MeshInstance3D under the rig
+#    (the mini-rig is body + head — single-mesh tint leaves part untinted),
+#    through ONE shared ShaderMaterial (no per-rig material clone).
+#  - play() ONLY on bind / intent change (re-play every frame restarts the clip +
+#    breaks phase); speed_scale=0 freezes HitStop; seek from the shared clock on
+#    (re)bind so a PR5 VAT->skeletal promotion does not pop to frame 0.
+#  - Unbound pool rigs: visible=false + process_mode=DISABLED (an idle
+#    AnimationPlayer in the tree still burns skinning cycles).
+#  - Missing/short render-state record -> skip the slot (no ghost rig; do NOT
+#    inherit the VAT adapter's live-full-health default for an expensive rig).
+#  - Headless: instantiate ZERO rigs / Skeleton3D / AnimationPlayer; the pool +
+#    cap bookkeeping still run (determinism fixtures stay asset-free, crash-free).
+
+const RIG_GLB_PATH := "res://assets/mini-characters/character-female-a.glb"
+const COLORMAP_PATH := "res://assets/mini-characters/Textures/colormap.png"
+const SHADER_PATH := "res://shaders/skeletal_horde.gdshader"
+const AGENT_STRIDE := 8
+
+# render-state record layout — MIRROR of density_feedback.rs RS_* offsets
+# (same contract HordeRenderAdapter consumes).
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const RS_FLAGS := 0
+const RS_HEALTH := 1
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const RS_INTENT := 3
+
+# AnimIntent — MIRROR of density_feedback.rs (locked by tests/anim_intent_parity.rs
+# against HordeRenderAdapter's identical consts; the wire contract).
+const ANIM_IDLE := 0
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_STAGGER := 2
+const ANIM_BALLISTIC := 3
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+const ANIM_ATTACK := 5
+const ANIM_INTENT_COUNT := 6
+
+var _render_root: Node3D
+var _headless := false
+var _cap := 0
+var _clocks: SlotClockBook
+var _ready := false
+var _enabled := false
+var _colormap_enabled := true
+var _health_tint_enabled := false
+
+var _mat: ShaderMaterial
+
+# pool (parallel arrays, indexed by pool slot 0.._cap-1)
+var _holders: Array[Node3D] = []  # carry the per-agent sim transform
+var _players: Array[AnimationPlayer] = []
+var _meshes: Array = []  # Array[Array[MeshInstance3D]] — body + head per rig
+var _pool_slot := PackedInt32Array()  # bound BufferSlot, -1 = free
+var _pool_intent := PackedInt32Array()  # cached AnimIntent, -1 = none (force play)
+
+# AnimIntent -> clip NAME resolved from the rig's own AnimationPlayer library.
+var _intent_clip: Array[StringName] = []
+
+# tunables (parity with the VAT horde so a PR5 swap does not jump scale/facing/feet)
+var character_scale := 1.0
+var y_offset := 0.0
+var facing_offset := 0.0
+
+# perf / bench instrumentation
+var last_assembly_usec := 0
+var last_live := 0
+
+
+func _init(render_root: Node3D, headless: bool, cap: int, clocks: SlotClockBook = null) -> void:
+	_render_root = render_root
+	_headless = headless
+	_cap = maxi(cap, 0)
+	# Shared clock: PR5 injects ONE book into both this adapter and the VAT
+	# adapter so swaps phase-match. PR4 defaults to its own (no shared live slots
+	# yet — dark path).
+	_clocks = clocks if clocks != null else SlotClockBook.new()
+
+
+# This is RefCounted but parents up to `cap` holder Node3Ds into the SceneTree
+# (_render_root). Godot nodes are manually managed, so they are NOT freed when
+# this RefCounted's last reference drops — a replaced/reconstructed adapter would
+# orphan its whole pool in the tree. Free the pool on predelete (GCA #600; the
+# .gemini/styleguide.md "nodes are not GC'd — always queue_free()" rule).
+func _notification(what: int) -> void:
+	if what == NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE:
+		for holder in _holders:
+			if holder != null and is_instance_valid(holder):
+				holder.queue_free()
+
+
+# Build the shared material + the rig pool + the intent->clip table. Returns false
+# (no-op) when headless or on a load failure. Idempotent.
+func setup() -> bool:
+	if _ready:
+		return true
+	if _headless:
+		# Headless: no rigs, but size the bookkeeping arrays so update()'s cap math
+		# + slot accounting still run crash-free (no Skeleton3D/AnimationPlayer).
+		_init_pool_state()
+		_ready = true
+		return false
+
+	var shader := load(SHADER_PATH) as Shader
+	if shader == null:
+		push_error("SkeletalHordeAdapter: failed to load shader")
+		return false
+	_mat = ShaderMaterial.new()
+	_mat.shader = shader
+	var cm := load(COLORMAP_PATH) as Texture2D
+	if cm != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("colormap", cm)
+	else:
+		push_warning("SkeletalHordeAdapter: colormap missing at %s — flat tint" % COLORMAP_PATH)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", _colormap_enabled and cm != null)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", _health_tint_enabled)
+
+	# Hoist the rig PackedScene load out of the per-rig loop (CR/Greptile #600):
+	# load() hits the resource cache after the first read, but the cast + dispatch
+	# still run N times. Load once, pass it into every _spawn_rig.
+	var rig_scene := load(RIG_GLB_PATH) as PackedScene
+	if rig_scene == null:
+		push_error("SkeletalHordeAdapter: failed to load rig scene at %s" % RIG_GLB_PATH)
+		return false
+	_init_pool_state()
+	for i in _cap:
+		if not _spawn_rig(i, rig_scene):
+			push_error("SkeletalHordeAdapter: failed to instantiate rig %d" % i)
+			return false
+	_build_intent_clip_table()
+	_ready = true
+	return true
+
+
+func _init_pool_state() -> void:
+	_pool_slot.resize(_cap)
+	_pool_slot.fill(-1)
+	_pool_intent.resize(_cap)
+	_pool_intent.fill(-1)
+	_holders.resize(_cap)
+	_players.resize(_cap)
+	_meshes.resize(_cap)
+
+
+# Instantiate one pooled rig: a holder Node3D (the sim-transform target) parenting
+# the rig .glb instance; cache its AnimationPlayer + every skinned MeshInstance3D;
+# assign the shared tint material to each mesh; start unbound (hidden + disabled).
+func _spawn_rig(i: int, packed: PackedScene) -> bool:
+	var rig := packed.instantiate() as Node3D
+	if rig == null:
+		return false
+	var holder := Node3D.new()
+	holder.name = "SkelRig%d" % i
+	if character_scale != 1.0:
+		rig.scale = Vector3(character_scale, character_scale, character_scale)
+	holder.add_child(rig)
+	_render_root.add_child(holder)
+
+	var player := _find_anim_player(rig)
+	var meshes: Array[MeshInstance3D] = []
+	_collect_meshes(rig, meshes)
+	# Tint every skinned surface (body + head) through the shared material — set on
+	# each mesh so no surface is left untinted (lc-codex). material_override
+	# replaces the GLB StandardMaterial, so the shader supplies the colormap.
+	for m in meshes:
+		m.material_override = _mat
+
+	_holders[i] = holder
+	_players[i] = player
+	_meshes[i] = meshes
+	holder.visible = false
+	holder.process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED
+	return true
+
+
+# Resolve each AnimIntent to a clip NAME present in the rig's AnimationPlayer, by
+# preference (first match wins; else the first available clip; else ""). Mirrors
+# HordeRenderAdapter._build_intent_clip_table but resolves against the live
+# AnimationPlayer library (the full Kenney set), not the sparse VAT bake table.
+func _build_intent_clip_table() -> void:
+	_intent_clip.resize(ANIM_INTENT_COUNT)
+	for i in ANIM_INTENT_COUNT:
+		_intent_clip[i] = &""
+	var player: AnimationPlayer = _players[0] if _players.size() > 0 else null
+	if player == null:
+		push_warning("SkeletalHordeAdapter: rig has no AnimationPlayer — clips unavailable")
+		return
+	var names := player.get_animation_list()
+	if names.is_empty():
+		push_warning("SkeletalHordeAdapter: AnimationPlayer has no clips")
+		return
+	var fallback := names[0]
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_IDLE] = _first_clip(names, ["idle", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION] = _first_clip(
+		names, ["walk", "run", "sprint", "locomotion"], fallback
+	)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_STAGGER] = _first_clip(names, ["hit", "stagger", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_BALLISTIC] = _first_clip(names, ["fall", "flail", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH] = _first_clip(names, ["die", "death", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_ATTACK] = _first_clip(
+		names, ["attack", "attack-melee-right", "punch", "walk"], fallback
+	)
+
+
+# A clip name may be bare ("walk") or library-qualified ("Library/walk"); match
+# on the unqualified suffix so either import naming resolves.
+func _first_clip(names: PackedStringArray, prefs: Array, fallback: StringName) -> StringName:
+	for pref in prefs:
+		for nm in names:
+			var bare := nm.get_slice("/", nm.get_slice_count("/") - 1)
+			if bare == pref:
+				return nm
+	return fallback
+
+
+func set_active(enabled: bool) -> void:
+	_enabled = enabled
+	# Dark path: visibility of bound rigs is managed per-frame in update(); when
+	# disabled, unbind the whole pool so no rig lingers.
+	if not enabled and _ready and not _headless:
+		for i in _cap:
+			_unbind(i)
+
+
+func is_active() -> bool:
+	return _enabled and _ready and not _headless
+
+
+func set_colormap_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_colormap_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", on)
+
+
+func set_health_tint_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_health_tint_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", on)
+
+
+# Per-frame: bind the first up-to-`cap` live agents to pool rigs (PR4's trivial
+# in-order selection — PR5's LODManager3D replaces it with a priority policy +
+# VAT dedup), drive each rig's transform / clip / freeze / tint, unbind the rest.
+func update(
+	agent_buf: PackedFloat32Array, render_state: PackedByteArray, count: int, delta: float
+) -> void:
+	if not _ready:
+		return
+	var t0 := Time.get_ticks_usec()
+	_clocks.ensure_capacity(count)
+	var max_agents := agent_buf.size() / AGENT_STRIDE
+	if count > max_agents:
+		count = max_agents
+	var rs_len := render_state.size()
+
+	var pool_i := 0
+	for s in count:
+		var bo := s * AGENT_STRIDE
+		var bxx := agent_buf[bo + 0]
+		var byx := agent_buf[bo + 1]
+		var ox := agent_buf[bo + 3]
+		var bxy := agent_buf[bo + 4]
+		var byy := agent_buf[bo + 5]
+		var oy := agent_buf[bo + 7]
+		# dead slots are zeroed by the snapshot writer -> all-zero basis
+		if absf(bxx) < 1e-9 and absf(bxy) < 1e-9 and absf(byx) < 1e-9 and absf(byy) < 1e-9:
+			_clocks.mark_dead(s)
+			continue
+		# Missing/short render-state record -> SKIP this slot (no ghost rig). An
+		# expensive skeletal rig must not render on a defaulted record, unlike the
+		# VAT adapter's cheap live-full-health fallback (lc-codex). Plain continue,
+		# NOT mark_dead: the slot is LIVE (it passed the zeroed-transform check),
+		# just record-less, so under a shared book (PR5) mark_dead would reset the
+		# clock that the VAT tier — which renders this slot via its fallback — is
+		# advancing, freezing/restarting its animation (GCA #600).
+		var ro := s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		if ro + RENDER_STATE_STRIDE > rs_len:
+			continue
+		if pool_i >= _cap:
+			# Over the LOD-0 cap — the VAT tier renders these slots and owns their
+			# clock. Plain continue, NOT advance: under a shared book (PR5) the VAT
+			# tier already advances them, so advancing here too would double-speed
+			# their animation; on a later promotion the skeletal tier seeks from that
+			# shared clock, which VAT has kept current (GCA #600).
+			continue
+
+		var health := render_state[ro + RS_HEALTH]
+		var hitstop := render_state[ro + RS_HITSTOP]
+		var intent := render_state[ro + RS_INTENT]
+		var clk := _clocks.advance(s, delta, hitstop)
+		var heading := atan2(bxy, bxx)
+		_bind(pool_i, s, ox, oy, heading, intent, health, hitstop, clk)
+		pool_i += 1
+
+	# Unbind the unused tail of the pool.
+	for i in range(pool_i, _cap):
+		_unbind(i)
+	last_live = pool_i
+	last_assembly_usec = Time.get_ticks_usec() - t0
+
+
+func _bind(
+	pool_i: int,
+	slot: int,
+	ox: float,
+	oy: float,
+	heading: float,
+	intent: int,
+	health: int,
+	hitstop: int,
+	clk: float
+) -> void:
+	if _headless:
+		# bookkeeping only (no nodes headless)
+		_pool_slot[pool_i] = slot
+		_pool_intent[pool_i] = intent
+		return
+	var holder := _holders[pool_i]
+	if holder == null:
+		return
+	# sim (X,Y) -> Godot (X,Z), Y=0 ground (+ feet offset); yaw about +Y from the
+	# sim heading — IDENTICAL convention to HordeRenderAdapter so VAT/skeletal
+	# facing matches at a PR5 swap.
+	holder.global_position = Vector3(ox, y_offset, oy)
+	holder.rotation.y = -heading + facing_offset
+	if holder.process_mode == Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED:
+		holder.process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_INHERIT
+		holder.visible = true
+
+	var player := _players[pool_i]
+	var clamped := clampi(intent, 0, ANIM_INTENT_COUNT - 1)
+	# play() ONLY on a (re)bind or intent change — never per frame (re-play
+	# restarts the clip). Seek to the shared clock so the clip phase-matches.
+	if player != null and (_pool_slot[pool_i] != slot or _pool_intent[pool_i] != clamped):
+		var clip := _intent_clip[clamped]
+		if clip != &"":
+			player.play(clip)
+			_seek_to_clock(player, clip, clk)
+	# Cache the intent OUTSIDE the player guard (CR/Greptile #600): a rig with no
+	# AnimationPlayer would otherwise never update the cache, keeping bind-state
+	# stale at -1 and re-entering the gate every frame. The clip table is static,
+	# so an unresolved (empty) clip can't appear later — no per-frame retry wanted.
+	_pool_intent[pool_i] = clamped
+	# HitStop = hold the pose (speed_scale 0); the shared clock is already frozen,
+	# so a later rebind seeks to the held phase.
+	if player != null:
+		player.speed_scale = 0.0 if hitstop > 0 else 1.0
+	# Per-instance health tint on EVERY skinned surface (body + head).
+	var h := float(health) / 255.0
+	for m in _meshes[pool_i]:
+		m.set_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac", h)
+	_pool_slot[pool_i] = slot
+
+
+func _unbind(pool_i: int) -> void:
+	if _pool_slot[pool_i] == -1:
+		return
+	_pool_slot[pool_i] = -1
+	_pool_intent[pool_i] = -1
+	if _headless:
+		return
+	var holder := _holders[pool_i]
+	if holder != null:
+		holder.visible = false
+		# DISABLED halts the AnimationPlayer tick + skinning for the idle rig
+		# (an in-tree AnimationPlayer still burns cycles otherwise — lc-agy).
+		holder.process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED
+
+
+# Seek a (looping) clip to the shared slot clock so phase is continuous across a
+# (re)bind — non-looping clips clamp at the end (a finished death holds its pose).
+func _seek_to_clock(player: AnimationPlayer, clip: StringName, clk: float) -> void:
+	var anim := player.get_animation(clip)
+	if anim == null or anim.length <= 0.0:
+		return
+	var t := (
+		fmod(clk, anim.length) if anim.loop_mode != Animation.LOOP_NONE else minf(clk, anim.length)
+	)
+	player.seek(t, true)
+
+
+func _find_anim_player(n: Node) -> AnimationPlayer:
+	if n is AnimationPlayer:
+		return n
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		var r := _find_anim_player(c)
+		if r != null:
+			return r
+	return null
+
+
+func _collect_meshes(n: Node, out: Array[MeshInstance3D]) -> void:
+	if n is MeshInstance3D:
+		out.append(n)
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		_collect_meshes(c, out)
+
+
+func debug_status() -> String:
+	if not is_active():
+		return "Skel3D: off"
+	return "Skel3D: %d/%d rigs, asm %.2fms" % [last_live, _cap, last_assembly_usec / 1000.0]

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/SlotClockBook.gd b/apps/game-godot/SlotClockBook.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..b3e81cc
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/SlotClockBook.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,70 @@
+class_name SlotClockBook
+extends RefCounted
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR4 — the shared per-BufferSlot animation clock.
+#
+# Extracted from HordeRenderAdapter's private `_clock`/`_was_live` arrays so the
+# VAT (render-LOD-1) and skeletal (render-LOD-0) tiers read the SAME accumulated
+# time per slot. PR5's phase-matched VAT<->skeletal swap is then automatic: a
+# promoted/demoted agent keeps its gait phase because both adapters seek from
+# clock(slot) at the swap. This class IS the cohort impl-preflight fold #2
+# (lc-codex / lc-agy / lc-gemini, 2026-06-22 — all three: extract now, not PR5).
+#
+# Render-owned phase ONLY — never enters the Rust ABI. The clock is presentation
+# interpolation derived from slot lifetime + HitStop, not deterministic sim
+# state; pushing it into the FFI would couple render interpolation into the
+# deterministic sim surface for no reason (lc-codex guard).
+#
+# Slot lifecycle (the caller drives, once per frame per slot):
+#   ensure_capacity(count)                  # grow backing arrays to >= count
+#   mark_dead(slot)                         # zeroed-transform / absent record
+#   advance(slot, delta, hitstop_remaining) # rebirth-reset + HitStop-freeze + tick
+#   clock(slot) -> float                    # the seconds for CUSTOM_DATA.x / seek
+#
+# Semantics are byte-for-byte the PR3 HordeRenderAdapter behavior (so its
+# frozen-slot bench assertion is the regression guard): a slot that was dead last
+# frame resets to 0 AND advances on the same rebirth frame; HitStop (remaining >
+# 0) skips the advance so the clip holds its current frame.
+
+var _clock := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _was_live := PackedByteArray()
+
+
+func ensure_capacity(count: int) -> void:
+	if _clock.size() >= count:
+		return
+	_clock.resize(count)
+	_was_live.resize(count)
+
+
+# Advance a LIVE slot. On the rebirth frame (was_live edge 0->1) the clock resets
+# to 0 first, THEN advances this frame — matching PR3 exactly (a reborn slot does
+# not skip a frame). HitStop freezes: remaining > 0 -> the clock holds. Returns
+# the post-advance clock for the caller's CUSTOM_DATA.x / AnimationPlayer.seek.
+func advance(slot: int, delta: float, hitstop_remaining: int) -> float:
+	if slot < 0 or slot >= _clock.size():
+		return 0.0
+	if _was_live[slot] == 0:
+		_clock[slot] = 0.0
+		_was_live[slot] = 1
+	if hitstop_remaining == 0:
+		_clock[slot] += delta
+	return _clock[slot]
+
+
+# Mark a slot dead (zeroed transform or absent render-state). The clock is not
+# zeroed here — it resets on the next rebirth via advance()'s was_live edge, so a
+# recycled BufferSlot (PR3 respawn) starts a fresh gait.
+func mark_dead(slot: int) -> void:
+	if slot >= 0 and slot < _was_live.size():
+		_was_live[slot] = 0
+
+
+func clock(slot: int) -> float:
+	if slot < 0 or slot >= _clock.size():
+		return 0.0
+	return _clock[slot]
+
+
+func was_live(slot: int) -> bool:
+	return slot >= 0 and slot < _was_live.size() and _was_live[slot] == 1

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/shaders/skeletal_horde.gdshader b/apps/game-godot/shaders/skeletal_horde.gdshader
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..cda2f69
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/shaders/skeletal_horde.gdshader
@@ -0,0 +1,50 @@
+shader_type spatial;
+render_mode unshaded;
+
+// ADR-029 P2b PR4 — skeletal render-LOD-0 tint shader.
+//
+// The LOD-0 sibling of horde_render.gdshader (the VAT/LOD-1 shader). It does
+// NOT animate vertices — the skinned Skeleton3D + AnimationPlayer pose the mesh
+// (Godot's built-in skinning runs before this vertex(); we never touch the
+// vertex position, so skinning is transparent). This shader exists only to
+// reproduce the VAT tier's FRAGMENT look — Kenney colormap + the squared-curve
+// damage flash — so an agent does not visibly pop colour when PR5 swaps it
+// between the skeletal and VAT tiers.
+//
+// Health is a per-instance `instance uniform`, set via
+// set_instance_shader_parameter() on EVERY skinned MeshInstance3D under the rig
+// (the Kenney mini-rig is body + head — a single-mesh tint would leave part
+// untinted; lc-codex impl-preflight catch). `instance uniform` lets every pooled
+// rig share ONE ShaderMaterial with a distinct per-instance value — no material
+// clone (lc-agy / lc-gemini confirm).
+
+uniform vec3 base_color : source_color = vec3(0.30, 0.55, 0.30);
+uniform vec3 hurt_color : source_color = vec3(1.0, 0.05, 0.05);
+uniform bool use_colormap = true;  // ON by default — match PR3's "real Kenney colors"
+uniform sampler2D colormap : filter_nearest, source_color;  // Kenney palette (UV lookup)
+// OFF by default so the clean Kenney textures show for the artist preview; the
+// canonical sim_driver._apply_health_tint mutator flips it (parity with the VAT).
+uniform bool use_health_tint = false;
+
+// Per-instance health fraction [0,1] — set per pooled rig via
+// set_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac", ...). Default full health so an
+// unbound/uninitialised rig never renders pre-reddened.
+instance uniform float health_frac = 1.0;
+
+varying float v_health;
+
+void vertex() {
+	// No vertex displacement — the engine skins the mesh. Just carry health to
+	// the fragment stage (read the instance uniform once, vertex-rate).
+	v_health = clamp(health_frac, 0.0, 1.0);
+}
+
+void fragment() {
+	vec3 body = use_colormap ? texture(colormap, UV).rgb : base_color;
+	// Squared curve so damage reads EARLY — identical to horde_render.gdshader so
+	// the two tiers match: v_health*v_health maps 0.7 -> 0.49 (~half red).
+	if (use_health_tint) {
+		body = mix(hurt_color, body, v_health * v_health);
+	}
+	ALBEDO = body;
+}

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/skeletal_horde_bench.gd b/apps/game-godot/skeletal_horde_bench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..8416f47
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/skeletal_horde_bench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,429 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR4 — skeletal render-LOD-0 cap-selection bench.
+#
+# Two phases, both on a REAL GPU (skeletal skinning is the GPU cost we are sizing;
+# headless cannot rasterise it):
+#   PHASE A (correctness, gates the exit code) — drives SkeletalHordeAdapter from a
+#     synthetic ABI (stride-8 transform + stride-4 render-state) and asserts the
+#     CPU-side contracts the disposable proof never had: in-order bind count, dead-
+#     slot skip, HitStop clock-freeze + speed_scale=0, intent->clip differentiation,
+#     per-instance tint distinctness, and root-motion drift < epsilon.
+#   PHASE B (cap selection, reports only) — an ISOLATION sweep N in {0,16,32,48,64}
+#     with vsync off, warmup then a sample window, collecting per-frame time;
+#     reports avg / p95 / p99 / max and picks the cap = highest N whose p95 MARGINAL
+#     frame time over the N=0 baseline stays within the budget. Writes a JSON summary
+#     + a PNG per N to user:// so the number is trackable across local setups
+#     (lc-agy fold — the bench can't run in CI).
+#
+# Run (real GPU, self-quits):
+#   <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://skeletal_horde_bench.tscn
+# Prints [skel-bench] PASS/FAIL + a VERDICT + the recommended cap, then quits
+# (0 = correctness clean, 1 = a correctness assertion failed).
+
+const CAP := 64  # pool sized to the max swept N
+const SPACING := 2.0
+const OUT_DIR := "user://skeletal_horde_bench/"
+
+# --- Phase A (correctness) ---
+const NC := 8  # synthetic agent count
+const FRAMES_C := 4  # fixed frames before asserting
+const DELTA_C := 0.1  # fixed delta so clock asserts are deterministic
+const DEAD_SLOT := 0
+const FROZEN_SLOT := 2  # hitstop > 0 -> clock must hold, speed_scale == 0
+const CONTROL_SLOT := 3  # hitstop 0 -> clock advances, speed_scale == 1
+
+# --- Phase B (perf sweep) ---
+const N_SWEEP := [0, 16, 32, 48, 64]
+const WARMUP := 20
+const SAMPLE := 60
+const BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS := 2.0  # cap = highest N with p95 marginal <= this (lc-agy)
+
+# render-state record layout (mirror of the ABI)
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+
+var _adapter: SkeletalHordeAdapter
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _xf := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _rs := PackedByteArray()
+
+var _phase := 0  # 0 = correctness, 1 = perf sweep, 2 = done
+var _frame := 0
+var _fails := 0
+var _watchdog := 0  # monotonic frame counter (hard anti-hang quit)
+
+# perf sweep state
+var _sweep_i := 0
+var _samples := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _results := []  # [{n, avg, p95, p99, max}]
+var _drift_start := Vector3.ZERO
+var _drift_rig := -1
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		# Headless can't rasterise skeletal skinning — verify the adapter no-ops
+		# without instantiating rigs, then quit clean (mirrors horde_render_bench).
+		var a := SkeletalHordeAdapter.new(self, true, CAP)
+		a.setup()
+		a.set_active(true)
+		var ok := not a.is_active() and a.last_live == 0
+		print("[skel-bench] headless — adapter no-op %s; quit 0" % ("OK" if ok else "UNEXPECTED"))
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+
+	DisplayServer.window_set_vsync_mode(DisplayServer.VSYNC_DISABLED)
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+	_cam.fov = 55.0
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+
+	_adapter = SkeletalHordeAdapter.new(self, false, CAP)
+	if not _adapter.setup():
+		print("[skel-bench] FAIL: adapter setup returned false")
+		get_tree().quit(1)
+		return
+	_adapter.set_active(true)
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(OUT_DIR))
+	print("[skel-bench] start; cap=%d clips=%s" % [CAP, str(_adapter._intent_clip)])
+	_build_buffers(NC)
+	_frame_camera(NC)
+
+
+func _process(delta: float) -> void:
+	# After _ready's headless quit(0), _process can still fire once before the tree
+	# tears down — _adapter is null on the headless path, so guard it (else a
+	# harmless-but-ugly "update on Nil" SCRIPT ERROR prints).
+	if _adapter == null:
+		return
+	# Hard watchdog: a self-quitting bench must NEVER hang a window on the
+	# operator's screen. Expected total ~404 frames (4 correctness + 5*(20+60)
+	# sweep); force-quit well past that if a phase ever stalls.
+	_watchdog += 1
+	if _watchdog > 1500:
+		push_error("[skel-bench] watchdog tripped at %d frames — force quit" % _watchdog)
+		get_tree().quit(2)
+		return
+	if _phase == 0:
+		_process_correctness()
+	elif _phase == 1:
+		_process_perf(delta)
+
+
+# --- Phase A: correctness ---
+
+
+func _process_correctness() -> void:
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, NC, DELTA_C)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < FRAMES_C:
+		return
+	_verify_correctness()
+	# advance to the perf sweep
+	_phase = 1
+	_frame = 0
+	_sweep_i = 0
+	_samples = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_begin_sweep_n()
+
+
+func _verify_correctness() -> void:
+	var live_expected := NC - 1  # one dead slot
+	_check(
+		_adapter.last_live == live_expected,
+		"bind count == %d live" % live_expected,
+		"bound %d, expected %d" % [_adapter.last_live, live_expected]
+	)
+
+	# HitStop: frozen slot clock held at 0; control advanced FRAMES_C*DELTA_C.
+	var frozen_clk: float = _adapter._clocks.clock(FROZEN_SLOT)
+	var control_clk: float = _adapter._clocks.clock(CONTROL_SLOT)
+	_check(frozen_clk == 0.0, "hitstop clock frozen at 0", "frozen clock = %.3f" % frozen_clk)
+	var expect := float(FRAMES_C) * DELTA_C
+	_check(
+		absf(control_clk - expect) < 1e-4,
+		"control clock advanced to %.3f" % expect,
+		"control clock = %.3f" % control_clk
+	)
+
+	# speed_scale: the frozen rig holds (0), the control runs (1).
+	var fp := _pool_index_of(FROZEN_SLOT)
+	var cp := _pool_index_of(CONTROL_SLOT)
+	if fp >= 0 and cp >= 0:
+		# Guard the player deref (CR #600): a rig that binds without an
+		# AnimationPlayer must record a controlled FAIL, not throw before _verify
+		# finishes.
+		var fp_player: AnimationPlayer = _adapter._players[fp]
+		var cp_player: AnimationPlayer = _adapter._players[cp]
+		if fp_player != null and cp_player != null:
+			var fss := fp_player.speed_scale
+			var css := cp_player.speed_scale
+			_check(fss == 0.0, "frozen rig speed_scale == 0", "speed_scale = %.2f" % fss)
+			_check(css == 1.0, "control rig speed_scale == 1", "speed_scale = %.2f" % css)
+		else:
+			_check(false, "frozen + control rigs have AnimationPlayers", "player missing")
+	else:
+		_check(false, "frozen + control rigs bound", "pool index missing (f=%d c=%d)" % [fp, cp])
+
+	# intent -> clip table: locomotion + death resolved + differentiate.
+	var loco: StringName = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION]
+	var death: StringName = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH]
+	_check(
+		loco != &"" and death != &"",
+		"locomotion + death clips resolved",
+		"loco='%s' death='%s'" % [loco, death]
+	)
+	_check(
+		loco != death, "intents differentiate (locomotion != death clip)", "both map to '%s'" % loco
+	)
+
+	# per-instance tint distinctness: two bound rigs with different health carry
+	# different instance shader params (catches material aliasing / single-mesh tint).
+	var p0 := _pool_index_of(1)  # first live slot
+	var p1 := _pool_index_of(CONTROL_SLOT)
+	var m0 := _meshes_of(p0)
+	var m1 := _meshes_of(p1)
+	if p0 >= 0 and p1 >= 0 and m0.size() > 0 and m1.size() > 0:
+		# Body + head: the multi-surface tint fold writes health_frac to EVERY
+		# MeshInstance3D under the rig. Assert >= 2 surfaces AND that the LAST
+		# surface carries the same per-instance value as the first — a body-only
+		# regression (the exact failure the fold guards) would slip past a >= 1 /
+		# [0]-only check (CR/Greptile #600).
+		_check(
+			m0.size() >= 2,
+			"rig has >= 2 skinned surfaces (body + head)",
+			"surfaces = %d" % m0.size()
+		)
+		var h0_first: float = m0[0].get_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac")
+		var h0_last: float = m0[m0.size() - 1].get_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac")
+		var h1_first: float = m1[0].get_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac")
+		_check(
+			h0_first == h0_last,
+			"all surfaces of a rig tinted (first == last)",
+			"first=%.3f last=%.3f" % [h0_first, h0_last]
+		)
+		_check(
+			h0_first != h1_first,
+			"per-instance tint distinct across rigs",
+			"both health_frac = %.3f" % h0_first
+		)
+	else:
+		_check(false, "tint-sample rigs bound with meshes", "missing meshes")
+
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[skel-bench] PHASE A: PASS (all correctness assertions)")
+	else:
+		print("[skel-bench] PHASE A: FAIL (%d assertion(s))" % _fails)
+
+
+# --- Phase B: perf sweep ---
+
+
+func _begin_sweep_n() -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[_sweep_i]
+	_build_buffers(n)
+	_frame_camera(maxi(n, 1))
+	_samples = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_frame = 0
+	_drift_rig = -1
+
+
+func _process_perf(delta: float) -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[_sweep_i]
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, n, delta)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame <= WARMUP:
+		# capture a drift baseline once warmed (first bound rig's root, walk sample)
+		if _frame == WARMUP and n > 0:
+			_drift_rig = _pool_index_of(0)
+			if _drift_rig >= 0:
+				_drift_start = _rig_root_local(_drift_rig)
+		return
+	_samples.append(delta * 1000.0)  # ms
+	if _frame < WARMUP + SAMPLE:
+		return
+
+	# sample window complete for this N
+	_results.append(_summarise(n, _samples))
+	_capture_png(n)
+	if n > 0 and _drift_rig >= 0:
+		var drift := _rig_root_local(_drift_rig) - _drift_start
+		var horiz := Vector2(drift.x, drift.z).length()
+		_check(
+			horiz < 0.05,
+			"N=%d root drift < 0.05 (in-place clip)" % n,
+			"root drifted %.3f m (root motion present?)" % horiz
+		)
+
+	_sweep_i += 1
+	if _sweep_i < N_SWEEP.size():
+		_begin_sweep_n()
+	else:
+		_finalise()
+
+
+func _finalise() -> void:
+	var baseline: float = _results[0]["p95"]  # N=0
+	var cap := 0
+	print("[skel-bench] PHASE B — isolation sweep (vsync off):")
+	for r in _results:
+		var marginal: float = r["p95"] - baseline
+		var within: bool = r["n"] == 0 or marginal <= BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS
+		if within and r["n"] > cap:
+			cap = r["n"]
+		print(
+			(
+				"[skel-bench]   N=%2d  avg=%.3f  p95=%.3f  p99=%.3f  max=%.3f ms  (marg p95 +%.3f)%s"
+				% [
+					r["n"],
+					r["avg"],
+					r["p95"],
+					r["p99"],
+					r["max"],
+					marginal,
+					"" if within else "  OVER"
+				]
+			)
+		)
+	print(
+		(
+			"[skel-bench] RECOMMENDED CAP = %d (p95 marginal <= %.1f ms over baseline)"
+			% [cap, BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS]
+		)
+	)
+	_write_json(cap, baseline)
+
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[skel-bench] VERDICT: GO — mechanism verified; cap=%d" % cap)
+	else:
+		print("[skel-bench] VERDICT: FAIL — %d correctness assertion(s)" % _fails)
+	_phase = 2
+	get_tree().quit(0 if _fails == 0 else 1)
+
+
+# --- helpers ---
+
+
+func _build_buffers(n: int) -> void:
+	_xf = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_rs = PackedByteArray()
+	_xf.resize(maxi(n, 1) * 8)
+	_rs.resize(maxi(n, 1) * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	var side := maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(maxi(n, 1))))), 1)
+	for i in n:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * SPACING
+		var gy := float(i / side) * SPACING
+		var ang := float(i) * 0.7
+		var cs := cos(ang)
+		var sn := sin(ang)
+		var o := i * 8
+		_xf[o + 0] = cs
+		_xf[o + 1] = -sn
+		_xf[o + 2] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 3] = gx
+		_xf[o + 4] = sn
+		_xf[o + 5] = cs
+		_xf[o + 6] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 7] = gy
+		var intent := ANIM_LOCOMOTION
+		var health := (i * 37) % 256
+		var hitstop := 0
+		if i == FROZEN_SLOT:
+			hitstop = 6
+		var flags := LIVE_BIT
+		var ro := i * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		_rs[ro + 0] = flags
+		_rs[ro + 1] = health
+		_rs[ro + 2] = hitstop
+		_rs[ro + 3] = intent
+	# correctness phase: make DEAD_SLOT a zeroed transform (skipped, not bound)
+	if n == NC:
+		for k in 8:
+			_xf[DEAD_SLOT * 8 + k] = 0.0
+		# give CONTROL_SLOT a distinct death intent so loco!=death differentiates
+		_rs[CONTROL_SLOT * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + 3] = ANIM_DEATH
+
+
+func _frame_camera(n: int) -> void:
+	var side := maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(n)))), 1)
+	var extent := float(side) * SPACING
+	var c := extent * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(c, extent * 0.95, extent * 1.45)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(c, 0.5, c), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+func _summarise(n: int, s: PackedFloat32Array) -> Dictionary:
+	var arr := s.duplicate()
+	arr.sort()
+	var cnt := arr.size()
+	var sum := 0.0
+	for v in arr:
+		sum += v
+	var avg := sum / maxf(float(cnt), 1.0)
+	return {
+		"n": n,
+		"avg": avg,
+		"p95": arr[mini(int(0.95 * cnt), cnt - 1)] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+		"p99": arr[mini(int(0.99 * cnt), cnt - 1)] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+		"max": arr[cnt - 1] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+	}
+
+
+func _write_json(cap: int, baseline: float) -> void:
+	var doc := {
+		"schema": "skeletal-cap-bench-v1",
+		"recommended_cap": cap,
+		"budget_marginal_ms": BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS,
+		"baseline_p95_ms": baseline,
+		"renderer": RenderingServer.get_video_adapter_name(),
+		"godot": Engine.get_version_info().get("string", "?"),
+		"viewport": [get_viewport().size.x, get_viewport().size.y],
+		"vsync": "disabled",
+		"sweep": _results,
+	}
+	var path := OUT_DIR + "skeletal_cap.json"
+	var f := FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.WRITE)
+	if f != null:
+		f.store_string(JSON.stringify(doc, "  "))
+		f.close()
+		print("[skel-bench] wrote %s" % path)
+	print("[skel-bench] JSON: %s" % JSON.stringify(doc))
+
+
+func _capture_png(n: int) -> void:
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := OUT_DIR + "skel_n%d.png" % n
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print("[skel-bench] capture %s" % path)
+
+
+func _check(ok: bool, label: String, fail_detail: String) -> void:
+	if ok:
+		print("[skel-bench] PASS: %s" % label)
+	else:
+		_fails += 1
+		print("[skel-bench] FAIL: %s — %s" % [label, fail_detail])
+
+
+func _pool_index_of(slot: int) -> int:
+	for i in _adapter._pool_slot.size():
+		if _adapter._pool_slot[i] == slot:
+			return i
+	return -1
+
+
+func _meshes_of(pool_i: int) -> Array:
+	return _adapter._meshes[pool_i] if pool_i >= 0 and pool_i < _adapter._meshes.size() else []
+
+
+func _rig_root_local(pool_i: int) -> Vector3:
+	var holder: Node3D = _adapter._holders[pool_i]
+	if holder == null or holder.get_child_count() == 0:
+		return Vector3.ZERO
+	var rig := holder.get_child(0) as Node3D
+	return rig.position if rig != null else Vector3.ZERO

diff --git a/.totem/specs/p2b-3d-horde.md b/.totem/specs/p2b-3d-horde.md
index 3a57673..eef5a22 100644
--- a/.totem/specs/p2b-3d-horde.md
+++ b/.totem/specs/p2b-3d-horde.md
@@ -131,3 +131,27 @@ The ADR-015 **"VAT anim-clock binds to per-instance `CUSTOM_DATA`, never global
 - **Skeletal cap** — the real unknown; the bench is the mitigation (don't pre-commit ~30–50).
 - **Death rendering** — deferred behind the linger/event contract; the ABI reserves room so it's non-breaking later.
 - **Mixamo license** — verify before lock-in.
+
+---
+
+## PR5 Implementation Design (built — `feat/592-p2b-pr5-lodmanager`)
+
+PR5 lands `LODManager3D` and closes mmnto-ai/liquid-city#592. Cohort impl-preflight (lc-codex / lc-agy / lc-gemini, 2026-06-22) converged 5/6 forks; the operator ruled the 6th (the combined bench rides in PR5). Built + verified — every bench VERDICT GO on the dev RTX 5070 Ti.
+
+### What it does
+- **`LODManager3D`** (Godot/render-owned `RefCounted`) owns BOTH horde adapters + the ONE shared `SlotClockBook`, and is the single facade `sim_driver` drives (`_lod_manager.update(camera, xf, render_state, count, delta)` — replaces the direct `_horde_adapter`). The render-ownership split holds (`sim_driver` does not grow a second renderer).
+- **Clock ownership is structural** (the heart of PR5): the manager is the SOLE writer to the shared book — one pass per frame, `mark_dead` a zeroed slot else `advance(slot, delta, hitstop)` for EVERY live slot regardless of tier (so an out-of-frustum / over-cap / VAT slot keeps a current gait phase → a promotion resumes continuously). Both adapters became pure clock READERS (`clock(s)`); their `update()` dropped `delta` so adapter-side `advance` cannot be reintroduced. That eliminates the GPU double-advance / reset class (GCA mmnto-ai/liquid-city#600 R3) by construction, not by discipline.
+- **Disjoint partition = the dedup:** a per-slot `_tier` (DEAD/VAT/SKEL) keyed by `BufferSlot`; each adapter renders only its tier. Liveness = nonzero transform, NOT `LIVE_BIT` (the ABI clears LIVE_BIT for `AnimIntent::Death`); death playback stays deferred.
+- **Priority + hysteresis:** out-of-frustum → VAT (instant, no debounce); else score by planar camera distance minus an activity bonus (HitStop / Stagger / Ballistic / Attack / low-health keep skeletal); re-seat existing SKEL within `exit_radius` first, then promote new candidates within `enter_radius` by score, bounded by `skeletal_cap` + a per-frame promote budget; a min-frames debounce gates promotion. Headless / camera-null → all-VAT, zero rigs. `Camera3D.is_position_in_frustum` is the frustum primitive.
+- **`HordeRenderAdapter` PREDELETE** added (parity with PR4's skeletal adapter; frees `_mmi`).
+
+### Resolved forks
+1. Clock ownership = **manager-owned** (structural). 2. Manager **owns both adapters**. 3. Priority = frustum + distance + activity + hysteresis; screen-projected size deferred. 4. Cutoff = **combined bench in PR5**; ships a conservative `@export skeletal_lod_cap = 32` (the combined render bench shows large headroom, below — 32 is conservative-with-room, tuned live vs the full frame). 5. Size = manager + partition + shared-book regression + combined bench + `HordeRenderAdapter` PREDELETE together (one fault surface); multi-fixture visual-parity PNG captures deferred to a fast-follow (they need new course authoring). 6. `HordeRenderAdapter` PREDELETE folded; `Render3DAdapter` / `VatHordeAdapter` left to a chore.
+
+### Verification (all VERDICT GO, exit 0)
+- **`lod_manager_bench`** — Phase A: the shared-clock transition regression (VAT→SKEL continuity, hit-stopped swap, over-cap, missing-record, dead-slot; exact-clock asserts) — runs HEADLESS too (the clock invariant is tier-independent → provable without a GPU). Phase B: combined N×M sweep picks the cap. On the RTX 5070 Ti @ N=256, all M∈{0,16,32,48,64} sit within the 2 ms combined-marginal budget (M=64 p95 +0.53 ms over all-VAT) — render headroom is large.
+- **`horde_render_bench`** / **`skeletal_horde_bench`** — both moved to the caller-owns-clock pattern, both still GO.
+- **Headless fixture smoke** — `sim_driver` boots the manager (dormant headless) without disturbing the determinism harness.
+
+### Deferred (tracked)
+Multi-fixture visual-parity PNG captures (fast-follow); sibling `Render3DAdapter` / `VatHordeAdapter` PREDELETE (chore); the LOD-0 shader **lighting** pass (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#586 — both tiers stay `unshaded`); death playback (the render-death contract, a later PR); export-safe VAT `.res` (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#599).
diff --git a/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd b/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd
index 868fd5b..609e5ce 100644
--- a/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd
+++ b/apps/game-godot/HordeRenderAdapter.gd
@@ -47,6 +47,12 @@ const RS_HEALTH := 1
 const RS_HITSTOP := 2
 const RS_INTENT := 3
 
+# LODManager3D tier id (mirror — the manager owns the canonical enum, ADR-029
+# P2b PR5). This adapter renders ONLY slots the manager assigned to the VAT

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/LODManager3D.gd b/apps/game-godot/LODManager3D.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..e49e50f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/LODManager3D.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,327 @@
+class_name LODManager3D
+extends RefCounted
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR5 — render-LOD manager (CLOSES mmnto-ai/liquid-city#592, the last
+# P2b PR). Owns BOTH horde render tiers + the ONE shared per-slot clock, and is
+# the single facade `sim_driver` talks to (Render-ownership split, ADR-029
+# decision 5 — sim_driver must not grow into one script driving two renderers).
+#
+# Each frame it partitions the live agent slots into:
+#   - render-LOD-0 (skeletal rig, SkeletalHordeAdapter) — the close / on-screen /
+#     high-action agents, where dismemberment is the high-payoff visual (Tenet 8);
+#   - render-LOD-1 (VAT MultiMesh, HordeRenderAdapter) — everything else.
+# The partition is DISJOINT (every live slot in exactly one tier) and that IS the
+# VAT<->skeletal dedup: no agent renders twice, and each slot's clock advances
+# exactly once.
+#
+# THE clock-ownership rule (the heart of PR5; folds GCA mmnto-ai/liquid-city#600
+# R3 + the cohort impl-preflight, lc-codex/lc-agy/lc-gemini 2026-06-22):
+#   The MANAGER is the SOLE writer to the shared SlotClockBook. It runs ONE pass
+#   per frame: mark_dead a zeroed slot, else advance(slot, delta, hitstop) — for
+#   EVERY live slot regardless of tier, so an out-of-frustum / over-cap / VAT slot
+#   keeps a current gait phase and a later promotion resumes continuously (lc-agy).
+#   Both adapters are pure READERS (clock(s)); they never advance/mark. With one
+#   shared book that structurally eliminates the double-advance / reset class the
+#   PR4 bench could not reach.
+#
+# Liveness = NONZERO transform in the snapshot, NOT `flags & LIVE_BIT`: the Rust
+# ABI clears LIVE_BIT for AnimIntent::Death while the transform is still nonzero
+# (density_feedback.rs agent_render_state_publish_system), so a dying-but-
+# positioned agent stays live + keeps its clock. Death PLAYBACK stays deferred
+# (lc-codex trap #2; the death-render contract is a later PR).
+#
+# Headless / no-camera: logical all-VAT, zero rigs. The manager still runs the
+# accounting + clock loop so determinism + the no-double-advance invariant are
+# testable without a GPU.
+
+const AGENT_STRIDE := 8
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const RS_HEALTH := 1
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const RS_INTENT := 3
+
+# AnimIntent mirror — only the "keep skeletal" activity classes (density_feedback.rs).
+const ANIM_STAGGER := 2
+const ANIM_BALLISTIC := 3
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+const ANIM_ATTACK := 5
+
+# Tier ids — the CANONICAL enum; HordeRenderAdapter.TIER_VAT / SkeletalHordeAdapter
+# .TIER_SKEL mirror these (parity comment in each adapter).
+const TIER_DEAD := 0
+const TIER_VAT := 1
+const TIER_SKEL := 2
+
+# Frustum test + planar-distance reference is taken at ~head height so an agent
+# at the screen edge resolves on its body, not its feet (lc-codex).
+const HEAD_Y := 0.9
+const FRAME_COUNTER_CEIL := 1_000_000  # clamp _tier_frames so it can't overflow
+
+var _vat: HordeRenderAdapter
+var _skel: SkeletalHordeAdapter
+var _clocks: SlotClockBook
+var _headless := false
+var _skeletal_cap := 0
+
+# per-slot tier state, keyed by stable BufferSlot. _tier = committed (previous
+# frame at the top of update); _next = this frame's assignment; _tier_frames =
+# frames the slot has held its committed tier (temporal debounce).
+var _tier := PackedByteArray()
+var _next := PackedByteArray()
+var _tier_frames := PackedInt32Array()
+
+# Tunables — live-tunable for the Camera3D-ADR pass. sim_driver exposes
+# skeletal_cap as @export; the radii/debounce defaults are documented here and
+# tuned on a live drive (and overridden by the bench for steady-state sweeps).
+var enter_radius := 18.0  # planar dist to camera below which a NEW agent is skeletal-eligible
+var exit_radius := 26.0  # planar dist above which an EXISTING skeletal agent demotes (dead-zone)
+var min_tier_frames := 8  # temporal debounce: frames in a tier before a distance-driven PROMOTE
+var per_frame_promote_cap := 4  # max promotions/frame — throttles the rig-activation spike
+var low_health_frac := 0.5  # below this health fraction, a small bonus to stay skeletal
+
+# perf instrumentation
+var last_skel := 0
+var last_vat := 0
+var last_select_usec := 0
+
+# per-frame scratch (member-held to limit allocation churn)
+var _cand := PackedInt32Array()  # live skeletal-candidate slots (in frustum)
+var _cdist := PackedFloat32Array()  # parallel: raw planar distance (radius test)
+var _cscore := PackedFloat32Array()  # parallel: dist - activity bonus (priority order)
+
+# selection scratch — member-held + reused via clear() so the per-frame hot path
+# stays allocation-free: Vector2(score, slot) elements are value types (no
+# nested-Array boxing) and the comparator is hoisted to ONE Callable (no per-frame
+# lambda alloc). CR + Greptile, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#601.
+var _keep_pairs: Array[Vector2] = []
+var _promo_pairs: Array[Vector2] = []
+var _score_cmp: Callable
+
+
+func _init(render_root: Node3D, headless: bool, skeletal_cap: int) -> void:
+	_headless = headless
+	_skeletal_cap = maxi(skeletal_cap, 0)
+	# ONE shared book injected into BOTH adapters — the whole point of PR5 (free
+	# phase-match on a swap). The manager is its only writer.
+	_clocks = SlotClockBook.new()
+	_vat = HordeRenderAdapter.new(render_root, headless, _clocks)
+	_skel = SkeletalHordeAdapter.new(render_root, headless, _skeletal_cap, _clocks)
+	# Hoist the sort comparator to one Callable (created once, reused each frame).
+	_score_cmp = _cmp_by_score
+
+
+# Build both adapters. Returns false only when the production VAT tier fails to
+# set up in a non-headless session (sim_driver warns). Skeletal returns false
+# under --headless by design — that is not a manager failure.
+func setup() -> bool:
+	var vat_ok := _vat.setup()
+	_skel.setup()
+	return vat_ok or _headless
+
+
+func set_active(enabled: bool) -> void:
+	_vat.set_active(enabled)
+	_skel.set_active(enabled)
+
+
+func is_active() -> bool:
+	return _vat.is_active()
+
+
+func set_health_tint_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_vat.set_health_tint_enabled(on)
+	_skel.set_health_tint_enabled(on)
+
+
+func set_colormap_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_vat.set_colormap_enabled(on)
+	_skel.set_colormap_enabled(on)
+
+
+# Read-only accessors for the bench / diagnostics (no privacy in GDScript, but
+# these keep the contract stable).
+func tier_of(slot: int) -> int:
+	return _tier[slot] if slot >= 0 and slot < _tier.size() else TIER_DEAD
+
+
+func slot_clock(slot: int) -> float:
+	return _clocks.clock(slot)
+
+
+# Per-frame entry point. `camera` drives frustum + distance (may be null →
+# all-VAT). Feeds both adapters the SAME stride-8 transform buffer + stride-4
+# render-state buffer the production horde consumes; the manager owns the clock.
+func update(
+	camera: Camera3D,
+	agent_buf: PackedFloat32Array,
+	render_state: PackedByteArray,
+	count: int,
+	delta: float
+) -> void:
+	# No is_active() gate here: sim_driver only calls update() when active (its own
+	# guard), and each adapter self-gates rendering (VAT no-ops when inactive,
+	# skeletal does headless bookkeeping only). Running the clock + tier accounting
+	# unconditionally lets the headless bench prove the clock-ownership invariant
+	# without a GPU — the whole point of the tier-independent regression.
+	var max_agents := agent_buf.size() / AGENT_STRIDE
+	if count > max_agents:
+		count = max_agents
+	_clocks.ensure_capacity(count)
+	_ensure_tier_capacity(count)
+	var rs_len := render_state.size()
+	var use_skel := (not _headless) and camera != null and _skeletal_cap > 0
+
+	var t0 := Time.get_ticks_usec()
+	_cand.clear()
+	_cdist.clear()
+	_cscore.clear()
+	var cam_x := camera.global_position.x if use_skel else 0.0
+	var cam_z := camera.global_position.z if use_skel else 0.0
+
+	# PASS 1 — classify dead/live, advance the ONE shared clock per live slot,
+	# gather in-frustum skeletal candidates.
+	for s in count:
+		var bo := s * AGENT_STRIDE
+		var bxx := agent_buf[bo + 0]
+		var byx := agent_buf[bo + 1]
+		var ox := agent_buf[bo + 3]
+		var bxy := agent_buf[bo + 4]
+		var byy := agent_buf[bo + 5]
+		var oy := agent_buf[bo + 7]
+		# Liveness = nonzero transform (NOT LIVE_BIT — see header / lc-codex #2).
+		if absf(bxx) < 1e-9 and absf(bxy) < 1e-9 and absf(byx) < 1e-9 and absf(byy) < 1e-9:
+			_next[s] = TIER_DEAD
+			_clocks.mark_dead(s)
+			continue
+
+		var ro := s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		var has_record := ro + RENDER_STATE_STRIDE <= rs_len
+		var hitstop := render_state[ro + RS_HITSTOP] if has_record else 0
+		# The single shared-clock advance for this live slot (HitStop-frozen),
+		# regardless of its eventual tier — so a promotion resumes continuously.
+		_clocks.advance(s, delta, hitstop)
+
+		# record-less live slot -> VAT (the cheap live-locomotion fallback; an
+		# expensive skeletal rig must never render a defaulted record, lc-codex #3).
+		if not has_record or not use_skel:
+			_next[s] = TIER_VAT
+			continue
+
+		# Out-of-frustum -> VAT, instant (leaving view is not churn — no debounce).
+		if not camera.is_position_in_frustum(Vector3(ox, HEAD_Y, oy)):
+			_next[s] = TIER_VAT
+			continue
+
+		# Skeletal candidate. Eligibility is RAW planar distance vs the radii; the
+		# activity bonus only reorders priority WITHIN the cap (a far attacker does
+		# not get pulled skeletal — it just wins ties among near agents).
+		var dist := sqrt((ox - cam_x) * (ox - cam_x) + (oy - cam_z) * (oy - cam_z))
+		var intent := render_state[ro + RS_INTENT]
+		var health := render_state[ro + RS_HEALTH]
+		var bonus := 0.0
+		if (
+			hitstop > 0
+			or intent == ANIM_ATTACK
+			or intent == ANIM_STAGGER
+			or intent == ANIM_BALLISTIC
+			or intent == ANIM_DEATH
+		):
+			bonus += exit_radius  # strong: keep close-action agents skeletal
+		if float(health) / 255.0 < low_health_frac:
+			bonus += enter_radius * 0.5
+		_cand.append(s)
+		_cdist.append(dist)
+		_cscore.append(dist - bonus)
+		# tier assigned in PASS 2 (selection); _next[s] is finalized there.
+
+	# PASS 2 — select the skeletal set with hysteresis (re-seat existing SKEL
+	# first, then promote new candidates by score, bounded by cap + flip budget).
+	_select_skeletal()
+
+	# Commit _next -> _tier, maintaining the in-tier frame counter for debounce.
+	for s in count:
+		if _next[s] == _tier[s]:
+			_tier_frames[s] = mini(_tier_frames[s] + 1, FRAME_COUNTER_CEIL)
+		else:
+			_tier_frames[s] = 0
+		_tier[s] = _next[s]
+
+	last_select_usec = Time.get_ticks_usec() - t0
+
+	# Drive both adapters with the committed, disjoint tier mask.
+	_vat.update(agent_buf, render_state, count, _tier)
+	_skel.update(agent_buf, render_state, count, _tier)
+	last_vat = _vat.last_live
+	last_skel = _skel.last_live if use_skel else 0
+
+
+# Select the skeletal occupants from the gathered candidates. Order matters
+# (lc-codex #5): re-seat existing SKEL that still satisfies the exit threshold
+# FIRST (sticky — avoids churn), THEN promote new candidates by score up to the
+# remaining cap and the per-frame promote budget. Everything else -> VAT.
+func _select_skeletal() -> void:
+	_keep_pairs.clear()  # (score, slot) — prev-SKEL still within exit_radius
+	_promo_pairs.clear()  # (score, slot) — new candidates within enter_radius, debounce-ok
+	for ci in _cand.size():
+		var s := _cand[ci]
+		var d := _cdist[ci]
+		var sc := _cscore[ci]
+		if _tier[s] == TIER_SKEL:
+			if d <= exit_radius:
+				_keep_pairs.append(Vector2(sc, float(s)))
+			# else: beyond the exit ring -> demote (falls through to VAT below)
+		elif d <= enter_radius and _tier_frames[s] >= min_tier_frames:
+			_promo_pairs.append(Vector2(sc, float(s)))
+	_keep_pairs.sort_custom(_score_cmp)
+	_promo_pairs.sort_custom(_score_cmp)
+
+	# Default every candidate to VAT; the selections below flip the winners to SKEL
+	# (cap-pressure / debounce / non-promoted candidates stay VAT). Writing _next
+	# directly avoids a per-frame `selected` Dictionary.
+	for ci in _cand.size():
+		_next[_cand[ci]] = TIER_VAT
+	# Re-seat existing SKEL first (sticky), then promote new candidates by score,
+	# bounded by the cap + the per-frame promote budget.
+	var n_skel := 0
+	for pair in _keep_pairs:
+		if n_skel >= _skeletal_cap:
+			break
+		_next[int(pair.y)] = TIER_SKEL
+		n_skel += 1
+	var promotes := 0
+	for pair in _promo_pairs:
+		if n_skel >= _skeletal_cap or promotes >= per_frame_promote_cap:
+			break
+		_next[int(pair.y)] = TIER_SKEL
+		n_skel += 1
+		promotes += 1
+
+
+# Sort comparator (hoisted to _score_cmp in _init): ascending by score (Vector2.x)
+# so the lowest-score = highest-priority candidates come first.
+func _cmp_by_score(a: Vector2, b: Vector2) -> bool:
+	return a.x < b.x
+
+
+func _ensure_tier_capacity(count: int) -> void:
+	# Resize to EXACTLY count every frame (not grow-only): on a count SHRINK the
+	# dropped slots' stale _tier / _tier_frames are discarded, so when count grows
+	# back a re-entered slot re-inits fresh (VAT, debounce 0) and must re-promote
+	# through enter_radius + min_tier_frames — it can never inherit a past life's
+	# SKEL tier or debounce counter and stale-promote without the throttle (GCA +
+	# Greptile, mmnto-ai/liquid-city#601). resize() to the current size is a no-op,
+	# so the steady-state path (count stable) costs nothing; range(old, count) is
+	# empty on a shrink and inits only the genuinely new slots on a grow.
+	var old := _tier.size()
+	_tier.resize(count)
+	_tier_frames.resize(count)
+	_next.resize(count)
+	for i in range(old, count):
+		_tier[i] = TIER_VAT
+		_tier_frames[i] = 0
+
+
+func debug_status() -> String:
+	if not is_active():
+		return "LOD3D: off"
+	return "LOD3D: %d skel / %d vat (sel %.2fms)" % [last_skel, last_vat, last_select_usec / 1000.0]

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/lod_manager_bench.gd b/apps/game-godot/lod_manager_bench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..193c4c1
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/lod_manager_bench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,411 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 P2b PR5 — LODManager3D verification + combined-budget cap bench.
+#
+# PHASE A — shared-clock transition regression (the test PR4 could NOT reach,
+#   because it needs BOTH tiers sharing ONE SlotClockBook — folds GCA
+#   mmnto-ai/liquid-city#600 R3 + lc-codex's prescribed scenario). Drives the
+#   real LODManager3D from synthetic buffers at a FIXED delta and asserts EXACT
+#   clocks across a VAT<->skeletal transition, a hit-stopped slot, an over-cap
+#   slot, a missing-record slot, and a dead slot. The clock invariants are
+#   TIER-INDEPENDENT (the manager advances every live slot's clock once,
+#   regardless of tier), so Phase A runs HEADLESS too — the no-double-advance /
+#   no-reset guarantee is provable without a GPU. The SKEL-tier assertions
+#   (which agent renders as a rig) are GPU-only and gated on `not headless`.
+#
+# PHASE B — combined cap sweep (real GPU only). Forces N_TOTAL agents through the
+#   manager with skeletal cap M in {0,16,32,48,64}; the cap picks the M closest
+#   in-frustum agents as rigs, the rest VAT — so each sample measures the TRUE
+#   production cost (VAT assembly + M skeletal rigs + manager scoring over N),
+#   not the PR4 ISOLATION ceiling. Picks the recommended cap from the combined
+#   p95 marginal over the M=0 baseline. JSON + one PNG per M to user://.
+#
+# Run (real GPU, self-quitting):
+#   <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://lod_manager_bench.tscn
+# Exit 0 = all Phase A assertions clean; 1 = a regression assertion failed.
+
+const DELTA := 0.1  # fixed so the Phase A clock asserts are exact
+const OUT_DIR := "user://lod_manager_bench/"
+
+# render-state record layout (mirror of density_feedback.rs)
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const RS_FLAGS := 0
+const RS_HEALTH := 1
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const RS_INTENT := 3
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+
+# tier ids (mirror of LODManager3D)
+const TIER_DEAD := 0
+const TIER_VAT := 1
+const TIER_SKEL := 2
+
+# Phase B combined sweep
+const N_TOTAL := 256  # total agents (upper-mid of the ~192-400 live envelope)
+const M_SWEEP := [0, 16, 32, 48, 64]  # skeletal cap M
+const WARMUP := 30  # frames to ramp the partition + settle a reconstruct teardown
+const SAMPLE := 60
+const SPACING := 1.6
+const BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS := 2.0  # cap = highest M whose p95 marginal <= this
+
+var _headless := false
+var _fails := 0
+var _watchdog := 0
+var _phase := 0  # 0 = regression, 1 = perf sweep, 2 = done
+
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _xf := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _rs := PackedByteArray()
+
+# Phase B sweep state
+var _manager: LODManager3D
+var _sweep_i := 0
+var _frame := 0
+var _samples := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _results := []  # [{m, avg, p95, p99, max}]
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	_headless = DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless"
+	if not _headless:
+		DisplayServer.window_set_vsync_mode(DisplayServer.VSYNC_DISABLED)
+		_cam = Camera3D.new()
+		_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+		_cam.fov = 55.0
+		_cam.current = true
+		add_child(_cam)
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(OUT_DIR))
+	# Headless: the camera-free clock invariant is fully testable now (all-VAT,
+	# zero rigs) — run the regression immediately + quit (no viewport needed).
+	if _headless:
+		_run_regression()
+		print(
+			"[lod-bench] headless — clock-ownership invariant only; quit %d" % (1 if _fails else 0)
+		)
+		get_tree().quit(1 if _fails > 0 else 0)
+
+
+func _process(delta: float) -> void:
+	if _headless:
+		return
+	_watchdog += 1
+	if _watchdog > 2000:
+		push_error("[lod-bench] watchdog tripped at %d frames — force quit" % _watchdog)
+		get_tree().quit(2)
+		return
+	if _phase == 0:
+		# Let the viewport size before the frustum test runs (frame 0/1 can be 0x0).
+		if _watchdog < 2:
+			return
+		_run_regression()
+		if _fails > 0:
+			print("[lod-bench] PHASE A: FAIL (%d) — skipping perf sweep" % _fails)
+			get_tree().quit(1)
+			return
+		print("[lod-bench] PHASE A: PASS (shared-clock regression)")
+		_phase = 1
+		_sweep_i = 0
+		_begin_sweep_m()
+	elif _phase == 1:
+		_process_perf(delta)
+
+
+# --- Phase A: shared-clock transition regression ---
+
+
+func _run_regression() -> void:
+	# Part 1 — one manager, cap large so cap pressure does not perturb the
+	# transition / hitstop / missing / dead asserts; over-cap gets its own part.
+	var mgr := _new_manager(8)
+	# Immediate, unthrottled transitions so the asserts are deterministic.
+	mgr.min_tier_frames = 0
+	mgr.per_frame_promote_cap = 64
+	mgr.enter_radius = 1.0e9
+	mgr.exit_radius = 1.0e9
+
+	# count=6, render-state covers only 5 records -> slot 5 is record-less.
+	var n := 6
+	_xf = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_xf.resize(n * 8)
+	_rs = PackedByteArray()
+	_rs.resize(5 * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	# slot 0 dead (zeroed). slots 1-5 live; positions set per frame below.
+	for s in range(1, n):
+		_set_xf(s, 0.0, 0.0)  # placeholder; positions assigned in the loop
+	# records (slot 5 has none)
+	_set_rs(1, 255, 0, ANIM_LOCOMOTION)  # transition slot — control-like clock
+	_set_rs(2, 255, 6, ANIM_LOCOMOTION)  # hitstop slot — clock must freeze
+	_set_rs(3, 255, 0, ANIM_LOCOMOTION)  # near control — clock must advance
+	_set_rs(4, 255, 0, ANIM_LOCOMOTION)  # filler near slot
+
+	# 6 frames; slot 1 starts BEHIND the camera (out of frustum -> VAT), then at
+	# frame 3 moves to the origin (in frustum, near -> SKEL). Every other live
+	# slot sits near the origin throughout.
+	for f in 6:
+		# dead slot 0 stays zeroed.
+		var slot1_behind := f < 3
+		_set_xf(1, 0.0, 25.0 if slot1_behind else 0.5)  # z=25 is behind cam (see _frame_cam)
+		_set_xf(2, 1.0, 0.0)
+		_set_xf(3, -1.0, 0.0)
+		_set_xf(4, 0.0, -1.0)
+		_set_xf(5, 1.5, 1.0)  # record-less live slot
+		if not _headless:
+			_frame_cam_origin()
+		mgr.update(_cam, _xf, _rs, n, DELTA)
+
+	var expect := 6.0 * DELTA  # 0.6
+	# Tier-INDEPENDENT clock invariants (run headless + GPU):
+	_check(mgr.slot_clock(0) == 0.0, "dead slot clock stays 0", "%.3f" % mgr.slot_clock(0))
+	_check(mgr.tier_of(0) == TIER_DEAD, "dead slot tier DEAD", "tier=%d" % mgr.tier_of(0))
+	_check(
+		absf(mgr.slot_clock(1) - expect) < 1e-4,
+		"transition slot clock continuous (%.2f, no reset on promote)" % expect,
+		"%.3f" % mgr.slot_clock(1)
+	)
+	_check(mgr.slot_clock(2) == 0.0, "hitstop slot clock frozen at 0", "%.3f" % mgr.slot_clock(2))
+	_check(
+		absf(mgr.slot_clock(3) - expect) < 1e-4,
+		"control slot clock advanced once/frame (no double)",
+		"%.3f" % mgr.slot_clock(3)
+	)
+	_check(
+		absf(mgr.slot_clock(5) - expect) < 1e-4,
+		"record-less slot clock advanced (VAT fallback, not mark_dead)",
+		"%.3f" % mgr.slot_clock(5)
+	)
+	_check(mgr.tier_of(5) == TIER_VAT, "record-less slot tier VAT", "tier=%d" % mgr.tier_of(5))
+	# SKEL-tier outcomes are GPU-only (headless forces all-VAT).
+	if not _headless:
+		_check(
+			mgr.tier_of(1) == TIER_SKEL,
+			"transition slot promoted to SKEL (near + in frustum)",
+			"tier=%d" % mgr.tier_of(1)
+		)
+		_check(mgr.tier_of(2) == TIER_SKEL, "hitstop slot SKEL", "tier=%d" % mgr.tier_of(2))
+		_check(mgr.tier_of(3) == TIER_SKEL, "control slot SKEL", "tier=%d" % mgr.tier_of(3))
+
+	# Part 2 — over-cap: cap=1, two equally-near live slots -> exactly one SKEL,
+	# one VAT, and BOTH clocks advance once/frame (the over-cap slot's clock is
+	# owned by the manager, not skipped or double-advanced).
+	var mgr2 := _new_manager(1)
+	mgr2.min_tier_frames = 0
+	mgr2.per_frame_promote_cap = 64
+	mgr2.enter_radius = 1.0e9
+	mgr2.exit_radius = 1.0e9
+	_xf = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_xf.resize(2 * 8)
+	_rs = PackedByteArray()
+	_rs.resize(2 * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	_set_rs(0, 255, 0, ANIM_LOCOMOTION)
+	_set_rs(1, 255, 0, ANIM_LOCOMOTION)
+	for f in 3:
+		_set_xf(0, 0.5, 0.0)
+		_set_xf(1, -0.5, 0.0)
+		if not _headless:
+			_frame_cam_origin()
+		mgr2.update(_cam, _xf, _rs, 2, DELTA)
+	var expect2 := 3.0 * DELTA  # 0.3
+	_check(
+		absf(mgr2.slot_clock(0) - expect2) < 1e-4,
+		"over-cap part: slot 0 clock advanced once/frame",
+		"%.3f" % mgr2.slot_clock(0)
+	)
+	_check(
+		absf(mgr2.slot_clock(1) - expect2) < 1e-4,
+		"over-cap part: slot 1 clock advanced once/frame",
+		"%.3f" % mgr2.slot_clock(1)
+	)
+	if not _headless:
+		var skel := int(mgr2.tier_of(0) == TIER_SKEL) + int(mgr2.tier_of(1) == TIER_SKEL)
+		var vat := int(mgr2.tier_of(0) == TIER_VAT) + int(mgr2.tier_of(1) == TIER_VAT)
+		_check(
+			skel == 1 and vat == 1,
+			"over-cap: exactly 1 SKEL + 1 VAT (cap=1)",
+			"skel=%d vat=%d" % [skel, vat]
+		)
+
+
+# --- Phase B: combined cap sweep ---
+
+
+func _begin_sweep_m() -> void:
+	var m: int = M_SWEEP[_sweep_i]
+	_manager = _new_manager(m)
+	# Steady-state cost at M skeletal: ramp instantly (no debounce / promote cap),
+	# cap is the only gate; radii huge so every in-frustum agent is eligible and
+	# the cap picks the M closest.
+	_manager.min_tier_frames = 0
+	_manager.per_frame_promote_cap = N_TOTAL
+	_manager.enter_radius = 1.0e9
+	_manager.exit_radius = 1.0e9
+	_build_grid(N_TOTAL)
+	_frame_cam_grid(N_TOTAL)
+	_samples = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_frame = 0
+
+
+func _process_perf(delta: float) -> void:
+	var m: int = M_SWEEP[_sweep_i]
+	_manager.update(_cam, _xf, _rs, N_TOTAL, delta)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame <= WARMUP:
+		return
+	_samples.append(delta * 1000.0)
+	if _frame < WARMUP + SAMPLE:
+		return
+	_results.append(_summarise(m, _samples))
+	_capture_png(m)
+	_sweep_i += 1
+	if _sweep_i < M_SWEEP.size():
+		_begin_sweep_m()
+	else:
+		_finalise()
+
+
+func _finalise() -> void:
+	var baseline: float = _results[0]["p95"]  # M=0 (all VAT)
+	var cap := 0
+	print("[lod-bench] PHASE B — combined sweep N=%d (vsync off):" % N_TOTAL)
+	for r in _results:
+		var marginal: float = r["p95"] - baseline
+		var within: bool = r["m"] == 0 or marginal <= BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS
+		if within and r["m"] > cap:
+			cap = r["m"]
+		print(
+			(
+				"[lod-bench]   M=%2d  avg=%.3f  p95=%.3f  p99=%.3f  max=%.3f ms  (marg p95 +%.3f)%s"
+				% [
+					r["m"],
+					r["avg"],
+					r["p95"],
+					r["p99"],
+					r["max"],
+					marginal,
+					"" if within else "  OVER"
+				]
+			)
+		)
+	print(
+		(
+			"[lod-bench] RECOMMENDED skeletal_lod_cap = %d (combined p95 marginal <= %.1f ms over all-VAT)"
+			% [cap, BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS]
+		)
+	)
+	_write_json(cap, baseline)
+	print("[lod-bench] VERDICT: GO — manager verified; combined cap=%d" % cap)
+	_phase = 2
+	get_tree().quit(0)
+
+
+# --- buffer / camera helpers ---
+
+
+func _new_manager(cap: int) -> LODManager3D:
+	var m := LODManager3D.new(self, _headless, cap)
+	m.setup()
+	m.set_active(true)
+	return m
+
+
+# Write a stride-8 Transform2D (identity basis + origin) for slot s. Heading is
+# irrelevant to the partition; the manager only reads the basis for liveness.
+func _set_xf(s: int, ox: float, oy: float) -> void:
+	var o := s * 8
+	_xf[o + 0] = 1.0
+	_xf[o + 1] = 0.0
+	_xf[o + 2] = 0.0
+	_xf[o + 3] = ox
+	_xf[o + 4] = 0.0
+	_xf[o + 5] = 1.0
+	_xf[o + 6] = 0.0
+	_xf[o + 7] = oy
+
+
+func _set_rs(s: int, health: int, hitstop: int, intent: int) -> void:
+	var ro := s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+	_rs[ro + RS_FLAGS] = LIVE_BIT
+	_rs[ro + RS_HEALTH] = health
+	_rs[ro + RS_HITSTOP] = hitstop
+	_rs[ro + RS_INTENT] = intent
+
+
+func _build_grid(n: int) -> void:
+	_xf = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_xf.resize(n * 8)
+	_rs = PackedByteArray()
+	_rs.resize(n * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	var side := maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(n)))), 1)
+	for i in n:
+		var gx := (float(i % side) - float(side) * 0.5) * SPACING
+		var gy := (float(i / side) - float(side) * 0.5) * SPACING
+		_set_xf(i, gx, gy)
+		_set_rs(i, (i * 37) % 256, 0, ANIM_LOCOMOTION)
+
+
+func _frame_cam_origin() -> void:
+	# Look at the origin from above/behind on +Z so a slot at z=+25 sits BEHIND
+	# the camera (out of frustum) while the origin cluster is in view.
+	_cam.position = Vector3(0.0, 7.0, 9.0)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+func _frame_cam_grid(n: int) -> void:
+	var side := maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(n)))), 1)
+	var extent := float(side) * SPACING
+	_cam.position = Vector3(0.0, extent * 0.95, extent * 1.1)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+func _summarise(m: int, s: PackedFloat32Array) -> Dictionary:
+	var arr := s.duplicate()
+	arr.sort()
+	var cnt := arr.size()
+	var sum := 0.0
+	for v in arr:
+		sum += v
+	return {
+		"m": m,
+		"avg": sum / maxf(float(cnt), 1.0),
+		"p95": arr[mini(int(0.95 * cnt), cnt - 1)] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+		"p99": arr[mini(int(0.99 * cnt), cnt - 1)] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+		"max": arr[cnt - 1] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+	}
+
+
+func _write_json(cap: int, baseline: float) -> void:
+	var doc := {
+		"schema": "lod-combined-cap-bench-v1",
+		"n_total": N_TOTAL,
+		"recommended_cap": cap,
+		"budget_marginal_ms": BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS,
+		"baseline_p95_ms": baseline,
+		"renderer": RenderingServer.get_video_adapter_name(),
+		"godot": Engine.get_version_info().get("string", "?"),
+		"viewport": [get_viewport().size.x, get_viewport().size.y],
+		"vsync": "disabled",
+		"sweep": _results,
+	}
+	var path := OUT_DIR + "lod_combined_cap.json"
+	var f := FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.WRITE)
+	if f != null:
+		f.store_string(JSON.stringify(doc, "  "))
+		f.close()
+		print("[lod-bench] wrote %s" % path)
+	print("[lod-bench] JSON: %s" % JSON.stringify(doc))
+
+
+func _capture_png(m: int) -> void:
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := OUT_DIR + "lod_m%d.png" % m
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print("[lod-bench] capture %s" % path)
+
+
+func _check(ok: bool, label: String, fail_detail: String) -> void:
+	if ok:
+		print("[lod-bench] PASS: %s" % label)
+	else:
+		_fails += 1
+		print("[lod-bench] FAIL: %s — %s" % [label, fail_detail])

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd b/apps/game-godot/PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd
