index 0000000..5e0378e
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,499 @@
+class_name PartSegmentedHordeAdapter
+extends RefCounted
+
+# ADR-029 gore epic mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565, PR1 (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#559) —
+# part-segmented render-LOD-0 horde adapter. Sibling of SkeletalHordeAdapter (SAME
+# public interface the LODManager3D LOD-0 tier expects) but a DIFFERENT mechanism:
+# the Kenney blocky kit is 6 rigid mesh parts (head/torso/arm-left/arm-right/leg-
+# left/leg-right) animated by an AnimationPlayer driving each part's TRS — NOT a
+# Skeleton3D+skin (import-proof: 6 meshes / 0 skins / 27 clips). Gore foundation:
+# dismemberment (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561) hides a part node; gibs
+# (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#560) sample a part's transform+mesh for SEPARATE debris —
+# NOT reparenting the live pooled part (breaks animation paths + pool reuse, lc-codex).
+# Fed the SAME render-state ABI as the VAT/skeletal horde; never queries the FFI.
+#
+# SCOPE (cohort impl-preflight folds, unanimous): single enemy type + a render-local
+# mesh-set seam (MESH_SETS; sim-provided at Phase 3) — NO Rust/ABI/stride change; NEW
+# sibling (not a SkeletalHordeAdapter re-tool) — manager owns clock PHASE, adapter
+# only READS clock(s) (LODManager3D clock-ownership/_tier invariant untouched).
+# Per-limb contract centralized HERE (BIT_TO_PART = ADR-027 Dismembered.parts bits);
+# reset_parts_visible() on recycle is load-bearing (no hidden-limb leak across pooled
+# agents). NON-goals: no gibs/dismember/death/lighting (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#586);
+# Pressure stays VAT (part-meshes LOD-0 ONLY); 2D untouched; mini rig = player/Specials.
+
+# Render-local mesh-set seam (lc-codex/lc-agy/lc-gemini): resolves an id -> asset.
+# Today one id -> one asset; the id becomes sim-provided at Phase 3 enemy variety
+# (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#562 / mmnto-ai/liquid-city#563 / mmnto-ai/liquid-city#564).
+# This is NOT a sim archetype — the sim publishes no archetype yet.
+const DEFAULT_HORDE_MESH_SET := "blocky-character-a"
+const MESH_SETS := {
+	"blocky-character-a": "res://assets/blocky-characters/character-a.glb",
+}
+# The blocky kit uses PER-CHARACTER textures (character-a -> Textures/texture-a.png),
+# NOT a shared colormap palette like the mini pack. Loaded into the shader's
+# `colormap` sampler (UV-mapped the same way). Couples to the mesh set; PR1 resolves
+# the single character-a texture.
+const BASE_TEXTURE_PATH := "res://assets/blocky-characters/Textures/texture-a.png"
+# Reuse the skeletal horde shader (UV base-texture + per-instance health_frac tint) —
+# the parts are MeshInstance3D, identical tint contract, no new shader needed.
+const SHADER_PATH := "res://shaders/skeletal_horde.gdshader"
+const AGENT_STRIDE := 8
+
+# render-state record layout — MIRROR of density_feedback.rs RS_* offsets.
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const RS_FLAGS := 0
+const RS_HEALTH := 1
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const RS_INTENT := 3
+
+# LODManager3D tier id (mirror — manager owns the canonical enum, ADR-029 P2b PR5).
+# NOTE: TIER_SKEL means "render-LOD-0 articulated rig", NOT necessarily a
+# Skeleton3D — this adapter is articulated by node-TRS, no skeleton. Same tier id.
+const TIER_SKEL := 2
+
+# AnimIntent — MIRROR of density_feedback.rs (the wire contract).
+const ANIM_IDLE := 0
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_STAGGER := 2
+const ANIM_BALLISTIC := 3
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+const ANIM_ATTACK := 5
+const ANIM_INTENT_COUNT := 6
+
+# Per-limb contract (Kenney verbatim names). BIT_TO_PART is ADR-027
+# Dismembered.parts bit order (bit0 head, bit1 arm-left, bit2 arm-right,
+# bit3 leg-left, bit4 leg-right, bit5 torso). mmnto-ai/liquid-city#560 /
+# mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561 read THIS map — do not scatter limb names elsewhere.
+const BIT_TO_PART: Array[StringName] = [
+	&"head", &"arm-left", &"arm-right", &"leg-left", &"leg-right", &"torso"
+]
+const PART_COUNT := 6
+
+var _render_root: Node3D
+var _headless := false
+var _cap := 0
+var _clocks: SlotClockBook
+var _mesh_set_id := DEFAULT_HORDE_MESH_SET
+var _ready := false
+var _enabled := false
+var _colormap_enabled := true
+var _health_tint_enabled := false
+
+var _mat: ShaderMaterial
+
+# pool (parallel arrays, indexed by pool slot 0.._cap-1)
+var _holders: Array[Node3D] = []  # carry the per-agent sim transform
+var _players: Array[AnimationPlayer] = []
+var _meshes: Array = []  # Array[Array[MeshInstance3D]] — ALL parts per rig (tint)
+var _part_by_bit: Array = []  # Array[Array[MeshInstance3D]] — bit-indexed (mask)
+var _pool_slot := PackedInt32Array()  # bound BufferSlot, -1 = free
+var _pool_intent := PackedInt32Array()  # cached AnimIntent, -1 = none (force play)
+
+# AnimIntent -> clip NAME resolved from the rig's own AnimationPlayer library.
+var _intent_clip: Array[StringName] = []
+
+# tunables (parity with the VAT/skeletal horde so a swap does not jump scale/feet)
+var character_scale := 1.0  # baked at spawn — set BEFORE setup() (post-setup no-op)
+var y_offset := 0.0
+var facing_offset := 0.0
+
+# perf / bench instrumentation
+var last_assembly_usec := 0
+var last_live := 0
+
+
+func _init(render_root: Node3D, headless: bool, cap: int, clocks: SlotClockBook = null) -> void:
+	_render_root = render_root
+	_headless = headless
+	_cap = maxi(cap, 0)
+	# Shared clock: the manager injects ONE book so VAT<->LOD0 swaps phase-match.
+	_clocks = clocks if clocks != null else SlotClockBook.new()
+
+
+func set_mesh_set(id: String) -> void:
+	# Forward-compat seam. Call BEFORE setup() — after setup() the pool already holds
+	# rigs built from the old asset, so a change would silently diverge (CR + Greptile).
+	if _ready:
+		push_warning("PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: set_mesh_set() after setup() ignored")
+		return
+	if MESH_SETS.has(id):
+		_mesh_set_id = id
+	else:
+		push_warning("PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: unknown mesh-set '%s' — using default" % id)
+		_mesh_set_id = DEFAULT_HORDE_MESH_SET
+
+
+func rig_glb_path() -> String:
+	return MESH_SETS.get(_mesh_set_id, MESH_SETS[DEFAULT_HORDE_MESH_SET])
+
+
+# RefCounted parenting nodes into the tree are NOT freed when the last ref drops —
+# free the pool on predelete (GCA mmnto-ai/liquid-city#600; styleguide rule).
+func _notification(what: int) -> void:
+	if what == NOTIFICATION_PREDELETE:
+		for holder in _holders:
+			if holder != null and is_instance_valid(holder):
+				holder.queue_free()
+
+
+# Build the shared material + the rig pool + the intent->clip table. Returns false
+# (no-op) when headless or on a load failure. Idempotent.
+func setup() -> bool:
+	if _ready:
+		return true
+	if _headless:
+		# Headless: no rigs, but size the bookkeeping arrays so update()'s cap math
+		# + slot accounting run crash-free (determinism fixtures stay asset-free).
+		_init_pool_state()
+		_ready = true
+		return false
+
+	var shader := load(SHADER_PATH) as Shader
+	if shader == null:
+		push_error("PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: failed to load shader")
+		return false
+	_mat = ShaderMaterial.new()
+	_mat.shader = shader
+	var cm := load(BASE_TEXTURE_PATH) as Texture2D
+	if cm != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("colormap", cm)
+	else:
+		push_warning(
+			"PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: texture missing at %s — flat tint" % BASE_TEXTURE_PATH
+		)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", _colormap_enabled and cm != null)
+	_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", _health_tint_enabled)
+
+	var path := rig_glb_path()
+	var rig_scene := load(path) as PackedScene
+	if rig_scene == null:
+		push_error("PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: failed to load rig scene at %s" % path)
+		return false
+	_init_pool_state()
+	for i in _cap:
+		if not _spawn_rig(i, rig_scene):
+			push_error("PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: failed to instantiate rig %d" % i)
+			return false
+	_build_intent_clip_table()
+	_ready = true
+	return true
+
+
+func _init_pool_state() -> void:
+	_pool_slot.resize(_cap)
+	_pool_slot.fill(-1)
+	_pool_intent.resize(_cap)
+	_pool_intent.fill(-1)
+	_holders.resize(_cap)
+	_players.resize(_cap)
+	_meshes.resize(_cap)
+	_part_by_bit.resize(_cap)
+
+
+# Instantiate one pooled rig: a holder Node3D (the sim-transform target) parenting
+# the rig .glb instance; cache its AnimationPlayer + every MeshInstance3D part;
+# resolve the bit->part map by node name; assign the shared tint material to each
+# part; start unbound (hidden + disabled).
+func _spawn_rig(i: int, packed: PackedScene) -> bool:
+	var rig := packed.instantiate() as Node3D
+	if rig == null:
+		return false
+	var holder := Node3D.new()
+	holder.name = "PartRig%d" % i
+	if character_scale != 1.0:
+		rig.scale = Vector3(character_scale, character_scale, character_scale)
+	holder.add_child(rig)
+	_render_root.add_child(holder)
+
+	var player := _find_anim_player(rig)
+	var meshes: Array[MeshInstance3D] = []
+	_collect_meshes(rig, meshes)
+	# Contract: the asset's mesh nodes ARE the 6 maskable limbs. If a future mesh-set
+	# carries accessory meshes they'd be tinted (via _meshes) yet invisible to the
+	# dismember mask (_part_by_bit) — warn so the divergence is loud at import (Greptile).
+	# mmnto-ai/liquid-city#560 / mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561 mask by _part_by_bit only.
+	if meshes.size() != PART_COUNT:
+		push_warning(
+			(
+				"PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: rig has %d meshes, expected %d parts — tint/mask may diverge"
+				% [meshes.size(), PART_COUNT]
+			)
+		)
+	# Tint EVERY part through the shared material (6 parts now, not body+head) —
+	# material_override replaces the GLB material so the shader supplies the colormap.
+	for m in meshes:
+		m.material_override = _mat
+
+	# Resolve the per-limb map by NODE NAME (robust to the import hierarchy — the
+	# runtime contract is scene-tree names, not glTF indices, lc-codex). A missing
+	# part is a hard contract failure surfaced for the import-proof bench.
+	var by_bit: Array[MeshInstance3D] = []
+	by_bit.resize(PART_COUNT)
+	for bit in PART_COUNT:
+		var part := _find_part(meshes, BIT_TO_PART[bit])
+		if part == null:
+			push_error(
+				(
+					"PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: rig missing part '%s' (bit %d)"
+					% [BIT_TO_PART[bit], bit]
+				)
+			)
+			# holder is parented into _render_root but not yet in _holders — free it
+			# here or it orphans/leaks on this abort (GCA; PREDELETE only walks _holders).
+			holder.queue_free()
+			return false
+		by_bit[bit] = part
+
+	_holders[i] = holder
+	_players[i] = player
+	_meshes[i] = meshes
+	_part_by_bit[i] = by_bit
+	holder.visible = false
+	holder.process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED
+	return true
+
+
+# Find a part MeshInstance3D by its (case-insensitive) node name among the rig's
+# collected meshes. Godot keeps the glTF mesh-node names on import.
+func _find_part(meshes: Array, part_name: StringName) -> MeshInstance3D:
+	var want := String(part_name).to_lower()
+	for m in meshes:
+		if String(m.name).to_lower() == want:
+			return m
+	return null
+
+
+# Resolve each AnimIntent to a clip NAME present in the rig's AnimationPlayer, by
+# preference. The blocky kit's clips: idle/walk/sprint/die/attack-melee-right/...
+func _build_intent_clip_table() -> void:
+	_intent_clip.resize(ANIM_INTENT_COUNT)
+	for i in ANIM_INTENT_COUNT:
+		_intent_clip[i] = &""
+	var player: AnimationPlayer = _players[0] if _players.size() > 0 else null
+	if player == null:
+		push_warning("PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: rig has no AnimationPlayer — clips unavailable")
+		return
+	var names := player.get_animation_list()
+	if names.is_empty():
+		push_warning("PartSegmentedHordeAdapter: AnimationPlayer has no clips")
+		return
+	var fallback := names[0]
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_IDLE] = _first_clip(names, ["idle", "walk", "static"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION] = _first_clip(
+		names, ["walk", "run", "sprint", "locomotion"], fallback
+	)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_STAGGER] = _first_clip(names, ["hit", "stagger", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_BALLISTIC] = _first_clip(names, ["fall", "flail", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH] = _first_clip(names, ["die", "death", "walk"], fallback)
+	_intent_clip[ANIM_ATTACK] = _first_clip(
+		names, ["attack-melee-right", "attack", "punch", "walk"], fallback
+	)
+
+
+# A clip name may be bare ("walk") or library-qualified ("Library/walk"); match on
+# the unqualified suffix so either import naming resolves.
+func _first_clip(names: PackedStringArray, prefs: Array, fallback: StringName) -> StringName:
+	for pref in prefs:
+		for nm in names:
+			var bare := nm.get_slice("/", nm.get_slice_count("/") - 1)
+			if bare == pref:
+				return nm
+	return fallback
+
+
+func set_active(enabled: bool) -> void:
+	_enabled = enabled
+	if not enabled and _ready and not _headless:
+		for i in _cap:
+			_unbind(i)
+
+
+func is_active() -> bool:
+	return _enabled and _ready and not _headless
+
+
+func set_colormap_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_colormap_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_colormap", on)
+
+
+func set_health_tint_enabled(on: bool) -> void:
+	_health_tint_enabled = on
+	if _mat != null:
+		_mat.set_shader_parameter("use_health_tint", on)
+
+
+# Make all parts of a pooled rig visible again. LOAD-BEARING: a pooled rig recycled
+# to a new agent must restore every limb before applying the new slot's mask, or a
+# limb hidden by mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561 leaks to the next agent (lc-codex).
+func reset_parts_visible(pool_i: int) -> void:
+	if _headless:
+		return
+	var by_bit: Array = _part_by_bit[pool_i]
+	for m in by_bit:
+		if m != null:
+			m.visible = true
+
+
+# Apply an ADR-027 Dismembered.parts mask: a SET bit hides that limb. PR1 only ever
+# applies mask 0 (all visible) — this establishes the seam + contract that
+# mmnto-ai/liquid-city#561 drives once the sim publishes Dismembered.parts.
+func _apply_part_mask(pool_i: int, parts_mask: int) -> void:
+	if _headless:
+		return
+	var by_bit: Array = _part_by_bit[pool_i]
+	for bit in PART_COUNT:
+		var m: MeshInstance3D = by_bit[bit]
+		if m != null:
+			m.visible = (parts_mask & (1 << bit)) == 0
+
+
+# Per-frame: bind the slots LODManager3D assigned to the render-LOD-0 tier
+# (`tiers[s] == TIER_SKEL`) to pool rigs, drive transform/clip/freeze/tint, unbind
+# the rest. The manager owns selection, the cap, dead-slot masking, the
+# record-less->VAT fallback, and the single shared-clock advance — this adapter is
+# a pure READER (no `delta`, never advances/marks the clock).
+func update(
+	agent_buf: PackedFloat32Array, render_state: PackedByteArray, count: int, tiers: PackedByteArray
+) -> void:
+	if not _ready:
+		return
+	var t0 := Time.get_ticks_usec()
+	var max_agents := agent_buf.size() / AGENT_STRIDE
+	if count > max_agents:
+		count = max_agents
+	var rs_len := render_state.size()
+	var tiers_len := tiers.size()
+
+	var pool_i := 0
+	for s in count:
+		if s >= tiers_len or tiers[s] != TIER_SKEL:
+			continue
+		if pool_i >= _cap:
+			# Defensive: the manager caps |LOD0| at _cap — never overrun the pool.
+			continue
+		var ro := s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		if ro + RENDER_STATE_STRIDE > rs_len:
+			# Defensive: the manager routes record-less live slots to VAT.
+			continue
+		var bo := s * AGENT_STRIDE
+		var ox := agent_buf[bo + 3]
+		var oy := agent_buf[bo + 7]
+		var bxx := agent_buf[bo + 0]
+		var bxy := agent_buf[bo + 4]
+		var health := render_state[ro + RS_HEALTH]
+		var hitstop := render_state[ro + RS_HITSTOP]
+		var intent := render_state[ro + RS_INTENT]
+		var clk := _clocks.clock(s)
+		var heading := atan2(bxy, bxx)
+		_bind(pool_i, s, ox, oy, heading, intent, health, hitstop, clk)
+		pool_i += 1
+
+	# Unbind the unused tail of the pool.
+	for i in range(pool_i, _cap):
+		_unbind(i)
+	last_live = pool_i
+	last_assembly_usec = Time.get_ticks_usec() - t0
+
+
+func _bind(
+	pool_i: int,
+	slot: int,
+	ox: float,
+	oy: float,
+	heading: float,
+	intent: int,
+	health: int,
+	hitstop: int,
+	clk: float
+) -> void:
+	if _headless:
+		_pool_slot[pool_i] = slot
+		_pool_intent[pool_i] = intent
+		return
+	var holder := _holders[pool_i]
+	if holder == null:
+		return
+	var is_recycle := _pool_slot[pool_i] != slot
+	# sim (X,Y) -> Godot (X,Z), Y=0 ground (+ feet offset); yaw about +Y — IDENTICAL
+	# convention to the VAT/skeletal horde so facing matches at a swap.
+	holder.global_position = Vector3(ox, y_offset, oy)
+	holder.rotation.y = -heading + facing_offset
+	if holder.process_mode == Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED:
+		holder.process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_INHERIT
+		holder.visible = true
+	# Recycle to a new agent -> restore all limbs (clears any prior agent's hidden
+	# state). Phase 3 adds `_apply_part_mask(pool_i, parts_mask)` here once the ABI
+	# carries the mask (it overwrites every part — no separate reset needed then).
+	if is_recycle:
+		reset_parts_visible(pool_i)
+
+	var player := _players[pool_i]
+	var clamped := clampi(intent, 0, ANIM_INTENT_COUNT - 1)
+	# play() ONLY on a (re)bind or intent change — never per frame (re-play restarts
+	# the clip). Seek to the shared clock so the clip phase-matches.
+	if player != null and (is_recycle or _pool_intent[pool_i] != clamped):
+		var clip := _intent_clip[clamped]
+		if clip != &"":
+			player.play(clip)
+			_seek_to_clock(player, clip, clk)
+	# Cache intent OUTSIDE the player guard (a rig with no AnimationPlayer must still
+	# advance the cache, else it re-enters the gate every frame).
+	_pool_intent[pool_i] = clamped
+	# HitStop = hold the pose (speed_scale 0); the shared clock is already frozen.
+	if player != null:
+		player.speed_scale = 0.0 if hitstop > 0 else 1.0
+	# Per-instance health tint on EVERY part.
+	var h := float(health) / 255.0
+	for m in _meshes[pool_i]:
+		m.set_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac", h)
+	_pool_slot[pool_i] = slot
+
+
+func _unbind(pool_i: int) -> void:
+	if _pool_slot[pool_i] == -1:
+		return
+	_pool_slot[pool_i] = -1
+	_pool_intent[pool_i] = -1
+	if _headless:
+		return
+	var holder := _holders[pool_i]
+	if holder != null:
+		holder.visible = false
+		holder.process_mode = Node.PROCESS_MODE_DISABLED
+
+
+# Seek a (looping) clip to the shared slot clock so phase is continuous across a
+# (re)bind — non-looping clips clamp at the end (a finished death holds its pose).
+func _seek_to_clock(player: AnimationPlayer, clip: StringName, clk: float) -> void:
+	var anim := player.get_animation(clip)
+	if anim == null or anim.length <= 0.0:
+		return
+	var t := (
+		fmod(clk, anim.length) if anim.loop_mode != Animation.LOOP_NONE else minf(clk, anim.length)
+	)
+	player.seek(t, true)
+
+
+func _find_anim_player(n: Node) -> AnimationPlayer:
+	if n is AnimationPlayer:
+		return n
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		var r := _find_anim_player(c)
+		if r != null:
+			return r
+	return null
+
+
+func _collect_meshes(n: Node, out: Array[MeshInstance3D]) -> void:
+	if n is MeshInstance3D:
+		out.append(n)
+	for c in n.get_children():
+		_collect_meshes(c, out)
+
+
+func debug_status() -> String:
+	if not is_active():
+		return "Part3D: off"
+	return "Part3D: %d/%d rigs, asm %.2fms" % [last_live, _cap, last_assembly_usec / 1000.0]

diff --git a/apps/game-godot/part_segmented_horde_bench.gd b/apps/game-godot/part_segmented_horde_bench.gd
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..59a1f32
--- /dev/null
+++ b/apps/game-godot/part_segmented_horde_bench.gd
@@ -0,0 +1,483 @@
+extends Node3D
+
+# ADR-029 gore epic mmnto-ai/liquid-city#565, PR1 (mmnto-ai/liquid-city#559) —
+# part-segmented render-LOD-0 horde bench (sibling of skeletal_horde_bench).
+# REAL GPU (headless instantiates zero rigs). PHASE A (gates exit): Godot
+# import-proof (6 parts resolve by NAME — the runtime contract, not glTF indices),
+# dismember hide/reset + pooled-recycle leak guard (lc-codex), bind count, HitStop
+# freeze, intent->clip, tint across all 6 parts, root planar-drift < eps. PHASE B
+# (reports): forced-N sweep {0,16,32,48,64} re-measures the cap (6 nodes/rig != the
+# skinned cost, lc-codex) + JSON + per-N PNG + a gameplay-camera perceptual baseline.
+# Run: <godot> --path apps/game-godot res://part_segmented_horde_bench.tscn
+#   0 = correctness clean, 1 = assertion failed, 2 = watchdog.
+
+const CAP := 64  # pool sized to the max swept N
+const SPACING := 2.0
+const OUT_DIR := "user://part_segmented_horde_bench/"
+
+# Phase A (correctness)
+const NC := 8  # synthetic agent count
+const FRAMES_C := 4  # fixed frames before asserting
+const DELTA_C := 0.1  # fixed delta so clock asserts are deterministic
+const DEAD_SLOT := 0
+const FROZEN_SLOT := 2  # hitstop > 0 -> clock holds, speed_scale == 0
+const CONTROL_SLOT := 3  # hitstop 0 -> clock advances, speed_scale == 1
+
+# Phase B (perf sweep)
+const N_SWEEP := [0, 16, 32, 48, 64]
+const WARMUP := 20
+const SAMPLE := 60
+const BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS := 2.0  # cap = highest N with p95 marginal <= this
+
+# render-state record layout (mirror of the ABI)
+const RENDER_STATE_STRIDE := 4
+const RS_HITSTOP := 2
+const LIVE_BIT := 0x04
+const ANIM_LOCOMOTION := 1
+const ANIM_DEATH := 4
+# tier ids (ADR-029 P2b PR5) — bench plays LODManager3D: live agents SKEL, dead DEAD.
+const TIER_DEAD := 0
+const TIER_SKEL := 2
+const PART_COUNT := 6
+
+var _adapter: PartSegmentedHordeAdapter
+var _cam: Camera3D
+var _xf := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _rs := PackedByteArray()
+# The adapter is a pure clock READER; this bench owns the tier mask + the shared
+# SlotClockBook (injected at construction; never reach into _adapter._clocks).
+var _tiers := PackedByteArray()
+var _book: SlotClockBook
+
+var _phase := 0  # 0 = correctness, 1 = perf sweep, 2 = perceptual, 3 = done
+var _frame := 0
+var _fails := 0
+var _watchdog := 0  # monotonic frame counter (hard anti-hang quit)
+
+# perf sweep state
+var _sweep_i := 0
+var _samples := PackedFloat32Array()
+var _results := []  # [{n, avg, p95, p99, max}]
+var _drift_start := Vector3.ZERO
+var _drift_rig := -1
+
+
+func _ready() -> void:
+	if DisplayServer.get_name() == "headless":
+		# Headless instantiates zero rigs — verify the adapter no-ops, then quit clean.
+		var a := PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.new(self, true, CAP)
+		a.setup()
+		a.set_active(true)
+		var ok := not a.is_active() and a.last_live == 0
+		print("[part-bench] headless — adapter no-op %s; quit 0" % ("OK" if ok else "UNEXPECTED"))
+		get_tree().quit(0)
+		return
+
+	DisplayServer.window_set_vsync_mode(DisplayServer.VSYNC_DISABLED)
+	_cam = Camera3D.new()
+	_cam.projection = Camera3D.PROJECTION_PERSPECTIVE
+	_cam.fov = 55.0
+	_cam.current = true
+	add_child(_cam)
+
+	_book = SlotClockBook.new()
+	_adapter = PartSegmentedHordeAdapter.new(self, false, CAP, _book)
+	if not _adapter.setup():
+		# setup() pushes an error naming any unresolved part (the runtime contract).
+		print("[part-bench] FAIL: setup returned false (see part-name push_error)")
+		get_tree().quit(1)
+		return
+	_adapter.set_active(true)
+	DirAccess.make_dir_recursive_absolute(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(OUT_DIR))
+	print("[part-bench] start; cap=%d clips=%s" % [CAP, str(_adapter._intent_clip)])
+	_build_buffers(NC)
+	_frame_camera(NC)
+
+
+func _process(delta: float) -> void:
+	if _adapter == null:
+		return
+	# Hard watchdog: a self-quitting bench must never hang a window (~404 frames).
+	_watchdog += 1
+	if _watchdog > 1500:
+		push_error("[part-bench] watchdog tripped at %d frames — force quit" % _watchdog)
+		get_tree().quit(2)
+		return
+	if _phase == 0:
+		_process_correctness()
+	elif _phase == 1:
+		_process_perf(delta)
+	elif _phase == 2:
+		_process_perceptual(delta)
+
+
+func _process_correctness() -> void:
+	_advance_clocks(NC, DELTA_C)
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, NC, _tiers)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < FRAMES_C:
+		return
+	_verify_correctness()
+	_phase = 1
+	_frame = 0
+	_sweep_i = 0
+	_samples = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_begin_sweep_n()
+
+
+func _verify_correctness() -> void:
+	var live_expected := NC - 1  # one dead slot
+	_check(
+		_adapter.last_live == live_expected,
+		"bind count == %d live" % live_expected,
+		"bound %d, expected %d" % [_adapter.last_live, live_expected]
+	)
+
+	var p1 := _pool_index_of(1)
+	_import_proof(p1)
+
+	# HitStop: frozen slot clock held at 0; control advanced FRAMES_C*DELTA_C.
+	var frozen_clk: float = _book.clock(FROZEN_SLOT)
+	var control_clk: float = _book.clock(CONTROL_SLOT)
+	_check(frozen_clk == 0.0, "hitstop clock frozen at 0", "frozen clock = %.3f" % frozen_clk)
+	var expect := float(FRAMES_C) * DELTA_C
+	_check(
+		absf(control_clk - expect) < 1e-4,
+		"control clock advanced to %.3f" % expect,
+		"control clock = %.3f" % control_clk
+	)
+
+	# speed_scale: frozen rig holds (0), control runs (1).
+	var fp := _pool_index_of(FROZEN_SLOT)
+	var cp := _pool_index_of(CONTROL_SLOT)
+	if fp >= 0 and cp >= 0:
+		var fpp: AnimationPlayer = _adapter._players[fp]
+		var cpp: AnimationPlayer = _adapter._players[cp]
+		if fpp != null and cpp != null:
+			_check(fpp.speed_scale == 0.0, "frozen speed_scale == 0", "= %.2f" % fpp.speed_scale)
+			_check(cpp.speed_scale == 1.0, "control speed_scale == 1", "= %.2f" % cpp.speed_scale)
+		else:
+			_check(false, "frozen + control rigs have AnimationPlayers", "player missing")
+	else:
+		_check(false, "frozen + control rigs bound", "pool index missing (f=%d c=%d)" % [fp, cp])
+
+	# intent -> clip table: locomotion + death resolved + differentiate.
+	var loco: StringName = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_LOCOMOTION]
+	var death: StringName = _adapter._intent_clip[ANIM_DEATH]
+	_check(
+		loco != &"" and death != &"",
+		"loco + death clips resolved",
+		"loco='%s' death='%s'" % [loco, death]
+	)
+	_check(loco != death, "intents differentiate (loco != death clip)", "both '%s'" % loco)
+
+	# per-instance tint across ALL 6 parts: first == last (none untinted) + distinct.
+	var m0 := _meshes_of(p1)
+	var m1 := _meshes_of(cp)
+	if p1 >= 0 and cp >= 0 and m0.size() > 0 and m1.size() > 0:
+		_check(
+			m0.size() == PART_COUNT, "rig has exactly 6 part surfaces", "surfaces = %d" % m0.size()
+		)
+		var h0_first: float = m0[0].get_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac")
+		var h0_last: float = m0[m0.size() - 1].get_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac")
+		var h1_first: float = m1[0].get_instance_shader_parameter("health_frac")
+		_check(
+			h0_first == h0_last,
+			"all 6 parts tinted (first == last)",
+			"first=%.3f last=%.3f" % [h0_first, h0_last]
+		)
+		_check(h0_first != h1_first, "tint distinct across rigs", "both = %.3f" % h0_first)
+	else:
+		_check(false, "tint-sample rigs bound with meshes", "missing meshes")
+
+	_verify_dismember_contract(p1)
+
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[part-bench] PHASE A: PASS (all correctness assertions)")
+	else:
+		print("[part-bench] PHASE A: FAIL (%d assertion(s))" % _fails)
+
+
+func _import_proof(pool_i: int) -> void:
+	if pool_i < 0:
+		_check(false, "import-proof rig bound", "no pool index")
+		return
+	var by_bit: Array = _adapter._part_by_bit[pool_i]
+	var all_live := by_bit.size() == PART_COUNT
+	for m in by_bit:
+		if m == null or not is_instance_valid(m):
+			all_live = false
+	_check(
+		all_live, "import-proof: all 6 bit->part nodes resolved + live", "by_bit=%s" % str(by_bit)
+	)
+	_check(_adapter._players[pool_i] != null, "rig has an AnimationPlayer", "player null")
+
+
+# Hide a subset via the mask, assert visibility; reset, assert all visible; then
+# RECYCLE the pool rig to a new agent slot and assert auto-reset — proving a hidden
+# limb cannot leak across pooled agents (lc-codex, load-bearing).
+func _verify_dismember_contract(pool_i: int) -> void:
+	if pool_i < 0:
+		_check(false, "dismember-contract rig bound", "no pool index")
+		return
+	var by_bit: Array = _adapter._part_by_bit[pool_i]
+	var mask := (1 << 0) | (1 << 1)  # hide head + arm-left
+	_adapter._apply_part_mask(pool_i, mask)
+	var hide_ok := true
+	for bit in PART_COUNT:
+		if by_bit[bit].visible != ((mask & (1 << bit)) == 0):
+			hide_ok = false
+	_check(hide_ok, "dismember: masked limbs hidden, others visible", "mask=%d mismatch" % mask)
+
+	_adapter.reset_parts_visible(pool_i)
+	var reset_ok := true
+	for bit in PART_COUNT:
+		if not by_bit[bit].visible:
+			reset_ok = false
+	_check(reset_ok, "reset_parts_visible restores all limbs", "a part stayed hidden")
+
+	# Re-hide, recycle to a fresh agent slot, assert the bind path restored visibility.
+	_adapter._apply_part_mask(pool_i, mask)
+	var new_slot: int = _adapter._pool_slot[pool_i] + 1000  # fresh id -> is_recycle
+	_adapter._bind(pool_i, new_slot, 5.0, 5.0, 0.0, ANIM_LOCOMOTION, 200, 0, 0.0)
+	var recycle_ok := true
+	for bit in PART_COUNT:
+		if not by_bit[bit].visible:
+			recycle_ok = false
+	_check(recycle_ok, "pooled recycle auto-resets limbs (no leak)", "a hidden limb leaked")
+
+
+func _begin_sweep_n() -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[_sweep_i]
+	_build_buffers(n)
+	_frame_camera(maxi(n, 1))
+	_samples = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_frame = 0
+	_drift_rig = -1
+
+
+func _process_perf(delta: float) -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[_sweep_i]
+	_advance_clocks(n, delta)
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, n, _tiers)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame <= WARMUP:
+		if _frame == WARMUP and n > 0:
+			_drift_rig = _pool_index_of(0)
+			if _drift_rig >= 0:
+				_drift_start = _rig_root_local(_drift_rig)
+		return
+	_samples.append(delta * 1000.0)  # ms
+	if _frame < WARMUP + SAMPLE:
+		return
+
+	_results.append(_summarise(n, _samples))
+	_capture_png(n)
+	if n > 0 and _drift_rig >= 0:
+		var drift := _rig_root_local(_drift_rig) - _drift_start
+		var horiz := Vector2(drift.x, drift.z).length()
+		_check(horiz < 0.05, "N=%d root drift < 0.05 (TRS clip)" % n, "drifted %.3f m" % horiz)
+
+	_sweep_i += 1
+	if _sweep_i < N_SWEEP.size():
+		_begin_sweep_n()
+	else:
+		_enter_perceptual()
+
+
+func _finalise() -> void:
+	var baseline: float = _results[0]["p95"]  # N=0
+	var cap := 0
+	print("[part-bench] PHASE B — isolation sweep (vsync off):")
+	for r in _results:
+		var marginal: float = r["p95"] - baseline
+		var within: bool = r["n"] == 0 or marginal <= BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS
+		if within and r["n"] > cap:
+			cap = r["n"]
+		print(
+			(
+				"[part-bench]   N=%2d  avg=%.3f  p95=%.3f  p99=%.3f  max=%.3f ms  (marg p95 +%.3f)%s"
+				% [
+					r["n"],
+					r["avg"],
+					r["p95"],
+					r["p99"],
+					r["max"],
+					marginal,
+					"" if within else "  OVER"
+				]
+			)
+		)
+	print(
+		(
+			"[part-bench] RECOMMENDED CAP = %d (p95 marginal <= %.1f ms over baseline)"
+			% [cap, BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS]
+		)
+	)
+	_write_json(cap, baseline)
+
+	if _fails == 0:
+		print("[part-bench] VERDICT: GO — mechanism verified; cap=%d" % cap)
+	else:
+		print("[part-bench] VERDICT: FAIL — %d correctness assertion(s)" % _fails)
+	_phase = 3
+	get_tree().quit(0 if _fails == 0 else 1)
+
+
+func _build_buffers(n: int) -> void:
+	_xf = PackedFloat32Array()
+	_rs = PackedByteArray()
+	_xf.resize(maxi(n, 1) * 8)
+	_rs.resize(maxi(n, 1) * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE)
+	var side := maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(maxi(n, 1))))), 1)
+	for i in n:
+		var gx := float(i % side) * SPACING
+		var gy := float(i / side) * SPACING
+		var ang := float(i) * 0.7
+		var cs := cos(ang)
+		var sn := sin(ang)
+		var o := i * 8
+		_xf[o + 0] = cs
+		_xf[o + 1] = -sn
+		_xf[o + 2] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 3] = gx
+		_xf[o + 4] = sn
+		_xf[o + 5] = cs
+		_xf[o + 6] = 0.0
+		_xf[o + 7] = gy
+		var health := (i * 37) % 256
+		var hitstop := 6 if i == FROZEN_SLOT else 0
+		var ro := i * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE
+		_rs[ro + 0] = LIVE_BIT
+		_rs[ro + 1] = health
+		_rs[ro + 2] = hitstop
+		_rs[ro + 3] = ANIM_LOCOMOTION
+	if n == NC:
+		for k in 8:
+			_xf[DEAD_SLOT * 8 + k] = 0.0
+		_rs[CONTROL_SLOT * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + 3] = ANIM_DEATH
+	_tiers.resize(maxi(n, 1))
+	for i in _tiers.size():
+		_tiers[i] = TIER_SKEL
+	if n == NC:
+		_tiers[DEAD_SLOT] = TIER_DEAD
+
+
+# Advance the shared clock once per slot, exactly as LODManager3D does (the adapter
+# is a pure reader): mark DEAD slots, advance live slots HitStop-frozen.
+func _advance_clocks(n: int, delta: float) -> void:
+	_book.ensure_capacity(n)
+	for s in n:
+		if _tiers[s] == TIER_DEAD:
+			_book.mark_dead(s)
+		else:
+			_book.advance(s, delta, _rs[s * RENDER_STATE_STRIDE + RS_HITSTOP])
+
+
+func _frame_camera(n: int) -> void:
+	var side := maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(n)))), 1)
+	var extent := float(side) * SPACING
+	var c := extent * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(c, extent * 0.95, extent * 1.45)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(c, 0.5, c), Vector3.UP)
+
+
+# Perceptual baseline phase: reframe to a low, close, combat-distance camera, let a
+# few real frames render so the move propagates, then capture + finalise — the
+# gameplay-distance legibility frame the cohort asked for. (A force_draw captured the
+# sweep overview as a stale frame; a real rendered frame is correct.)
+func _enter_perceptual() -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[N_SWEEP.size() - 1]
+	_build_buffers(n)
+	var c := float(maxi(int(ceil(sqrt(float(n)))), 1)) * SPACING * 0.5
+	_cam.position = Vector3(c, 6.0, c + 9.0)
+	_cam.look_at(Vector3(c, 0.8, c), Vector3.UP)
+	_phase = 2
+	_frame = 0
+
+
+func _process_perceptual(delta: float) -> void:
+	var n: int = N_SWEEP[N_SWEEP.size() - 1]
+	_advance_clocks(n, delta)
+	_adapter.update(_xf, _rs, n, _tiers)
+	_frame += 1
+	if _frame < 4:
+		return
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := OUT_DIR + "perceptual_gameplay_cam.png"
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print("[part-bench] perceptual baseline capture %s" % path)
+	_finalise()
+
+
+func _summarise(n: int, s: PackedFloat32Array) -> Dictionary:
+	var arr := s.duplicate()
+	arr.sort()
+	var cnt := arr.size()
+	var sum := 0.0
+	for v in arr:
+		sum += v
+	return {
+		"n": n,
+		"avg": sum / maxf(float(cnt), 1.0),
+		"p95": arr[mini(int(0.95 * cnt), cnt - 1)] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+		"p99": arr[mini(int(0.99 * cnt), cnt - 1)] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+		"max": arr[cnt - 1] if cnt > 0 else 0.0,
+	}
+
+
+func _write_json(cap: int, baseline: float) -> void:
+	var doc := {
+		"schema": "part-segmented-cap-bench-v1",
+		"recommended_cap": cap,
+		"budget_marginal_ms": BUDGET_MARGINAL_MS,
+		"baseline_p95_ms": baseline,
+		"renderer": RenderingServer.get_video_adapter_name(),
+		"godot": Engine.get_version_info().get("string", "?"),
+		"viewport": [get_viewport().size.x, get_viewport().size.y],
+		"vsync": "disabled",
+		"sweep": _results,
+	}
+	var path := OUT_DIR + "part_segmented_cap.json"
+	var f := FileAccess.open(path, FileAccess.WRITE)
+	if f != null:
+		f.store_string(JSON.stringify(doc, "  "))
+		f.close()
+		print("[part-bench] wrote %s" % path)
+	print("[part-bench] JSON: %s" % JSON.stringify(doc))
+
+
+func _capture_png(n: int) -> void:
+	var img := get_viewport().get_texture().get_image()
+	if img != null:
+		var path := OUT_DIR + "part_n%d.png" % n
+		img.save_png(ProjectSettings.globalize_path(path))
+		print("[part-bench] capture %s" % path)
+
+
+func _check(ok: bool, label: String, fail_detail: String) -> void:
+	if ok:
+		print("[part-bench] PASS: %s" % label)
+	else:
+		_fails += 1
+		print("[part-bench] FAIL: %s — %s" % [label, fail_detail])
+
+
+func _pool_index_of(slot: int) -> int:
+	for i in _adapter._pool_slot.size():
+		if _adapter._pool_slot[i] == slot:
+			return i
+	return -1
+
+
+func _meshes_of(pool_i: int) -> Array:
+	return _adapter._meshes[pool_i] if pool_i >= 0 and pool_i < _adapter._meshes.size() else []
+
+
+func _rig_root_local(pool_i: int) -> Vector3:
+	var holder: Node3D = _adapter._holders[pool_i]
+	if holder == null or holder.get_child_count() == 0:
+		return Vector3.ZERO
+	var rig := holder.get_child(0) as Node3D
+	return rig.position if rig != null else Vector3.ZERO
